commit.message:
feat(freeze): add dispatch table mode for relooper codegen

Add dispatch table mode to relooper-based code generation:
- Functions with >0 blocks use runtime dispatch tables instead of comptime switch
- This prevents Zig comptime monomorphization explosion for large codebases like TSC
- Block functions return BlockResult tagged union (.next_block, .return_value, etc.)
- Async functions excluded from dispatch mode (need async state in main function)

Also:
- Remove debug prints that slow down compilation
- Fix various dispatch_mode return type issues (put_loc_check, throw, check_ctor, etc.)

feat(mux): embed web assets, use dialog for preview, faster shutdown

- Embed index.html and client.js directly into binary (~140KB)
- Replace browser confirm() with custom preview dialog
- Use non-blocking sockets for immediate Ctrl+C shutdown
- Integrate bun build into zig build for single-command builds

perf(mux): optimize encoder and embed web assets

- Add PrioritizeEncodingSpeedOverQuality for faster encoding
- Embed web assets (index.html, client.js) into binary (~140KB)
- Remove --web-root CLI arg, simplify deployment
- Add NV12 buffer infrastructure for future YUV optimization
- Keep IOSurface locked during encode for GPU sync
- Confirmed BGRA copy path faster than direct IOSurface (26 vs 10 FPS)
chore(mux): link Accelerate framework, update README shortcuts

perf(encoder): optimize video encoding for lower latency

- Use faster scaling interpolation (no high quality resampling)
- Increase keyframe interval to 120 frames (4s) for less overhead
- Add IOSurface include for future zero-copy encoding
- Add CVPixelBufferPool infrastructure (unused for now)

feat(mux): implement download preview (dry-run) feature

- Add preview message handlers (0x17, 0x18) on server
- Handle preview responses (0x19, 0x1A) on client
- Show confirm dialog with file info before download
- Enable preview checkbox (checked by default)

fix(viewer): clear terminal text when navigating from error page

Error page text was persisting in terminal buffer and showing through
hint overlays. Now clear terminal content area when:
- Switching tabs (clearContentArea)
- Navigating via Ctrl+L from error/blank page

feat(viewer): show error page for invalid URLs, reduce min viewport size

- Add renderErrorPage() to show friendly error when page fails to load
  instead of crashing with ScreencastTimeout
- User can press Ctrl+L to try another URL or Ctrl+Q to quit
- Reduce MIN_WIDTH/MIN_HEIGHT from 800x600 to 100x100 to fix
  viewport scaling on narrow terminals (was wasting ~23% height)
- Add handleBinaryFilePreview/handleBinaryFolderPreview to mux server
- Optimize mux render loop with direct IOSurface encoding
fix(web): disable preview checkboxes until server implements them

fix(web): preserve flex style when converting leaf to split

When a leaf container is split, rebuildDOM() creates a new element.
The old element had a flex style set by its parent's applyRatio().
Without preserving this, the new split container collapses and
sibling panels disappear.

fix(web): revert download message to old format (server compat)

Server doesn't support the new options yet. Keep the dialog reading
options but send the old message format. Options are logged to console.

feat(web): implement download dialog options (preview, delete, excludes)

DownloadDialog now reads and passes rsync-style options:
- Preview/dry-run mode
- Delete extra files
- Exclude patterns

Updated binary protocol to include flags and exclude patterns.

fix(web): add mobile styles for submenu dropdowns

On mobile/touch devices, submenu dropdowns now:
- Use static positioning (inline with menu)
- Show/hide with open class on tap
- Use down/up arrows instead of left arrow
fix(web): add scroll to mobile menubar, not individual dropdowns
fix(web): use direct child selector for main menu scroll
fix(web): only scroll main menu dropdown, not submenus

fix(web): make menu dropdown scrollable when too long

Add max-height and overflow-y to prevent menu from extending past
the bottom of the viewport on mobile devices.

fix(web): equalize considers split direction like Ghostty

When equalizing, a split with a different direction counts as weight 1,
not the sum of its children. This ensures 3 columns stay 33%/33%/33%
even if one column has vertical sub-splits.

Also fix updateSplitMenuVisibility error (tab.panels doesn't exist).

fix(web): equalize splits using weight-based ratio like Ghostty

Instead of setting all ratios to 0.5, calculate the ratio based on
the number of leaf panels on each side. This ensures 3 columns get
33%/33%/33% instead of 50%/25%/25%.

For example, with panels A|B|C:
- First split: weight_left=1, weight_right=2, ratio=1/3
- Second split: weight_left=1, weight_right=1, ratio=1/2
Result: A=33%, B=33%, C=33%

fix(web): fix submenu hover gap causing premature close

Use padding instead of margin to create visual separation between
parent menu and submenu dropdown. This maintains a continuous hover
area so the submenu stays open when moving the mouse to it.

fix(web): fix split selection shortcuts to match menu

- Change ⌘⌥Arrow to ⌘⇧Arrow for directional split selection (matches menu)
- Use selectSplitDirection instead of navigateSplit for consistency
- Use selectPreviousSplit/selectNextSplit for ⌘[/⌘] shortcuts
- Remove unused navigateSplit and cycleSplit methods

feat(mux): persist active panel per tab

- Add active_panel_id field to Tab struct on server
- Update focus_panel handler to track which panel is active in each tab
- Serialize activePanelId in layout JSON
- Restore active panel when loading layout from server
- Clear active_panel_id when that panel is removed

fix(mux): correctly parse split direction from client

The client sends "right"/"left" for horizontal splits and "down"/"up"
for vertical splits, but the server was only checking for "vertical"
and defaulting to horizontal. This caused vertical splits to be
restored as horizontal after reload.

feat(web): implement split selection, resize, and equalize methods

Add methods to SplitContainer:
- selectSplitInDirection: find panel in given direction using spatial coords
- equalize: recursively set all split ratios to 50%
- resizeSplit: move divider in given direction by specified amount

Update App to use these methods for Window menu actions.

fix(web): use direct child selector for menu dropdown visibility

The rule .menu:hover .menu-dropdown was showing all nested submenu
dropdowns when hovering the parent menu. Changed to direct child
selector (>) so only the immediate dropdown is shown, not nested ones.

fix(web): keep split submenu dropdowns hidden until hover/touch

- Add specific CSS rules to ensure split submenu dropdowns stay hidden
- Only adjust submenu position when dropdown is actually visible
- Use mouseover event for position adjustment to catch visibility changes
fix(web): fix CSS specificity for split menu item visibility
fix(web): add max-height and scroll to menu dropdowns

fix(web): hide split menu items by default and fix submenu positioning

- Hide split-related menu items (Zoom Split, Select/Resize Split) when
  there's only one panel in the tab
- Add dynamic submenu positioning to avoid going off left or bottom edge
- Show split menu items only when tab has multiple panels
fix(web): align submenu to bottom so it opens upward

fix(web): change split navigation shortcuts to ⌘⇧[] to avoid conflicts

⌘[ and ⌘] conflict with browser history navigation. Changed to ⌘⇧[
and ⌘⇧] for selecting previous/next split.

refactor(web): use hover media query instead of width for touch detection

Change CSS media queries from max-width: 768px to hover: none for better
touch device detection. This correctly identifies touch-only devices
regardless of screen size.
fix(mux): submenu hover on desktop, tap on touch devices
fix(mux): position submenu on left side (menu aligned right)
feat(mux): dim non-focused panels in split view
fix(mux): add fullscreen shortcut to menu, format README

docs(mux): update README with latest features

- H.264 video streaming architecture (not WebGPU)
- Window menu with fullscreen, zoom split, tab/split navigation
- File transfer with rsync-like options
- Updated keyboard shortcuts and menus
- Debug mode documentation
- TypeScript web client build instructions

feat(mux): add Window menu with tab/split navigation

- Toggle Full Screen (Cmd+Shift+F)
- Show Previous/Next Tab (Ctrl+Shift+Tab / Ctrl+Tab)
- Zoom Split (Cmd+Shift+Enter)
- Select Previous/Next Split (Cmd+[ / Cmd+])
- Select Split submenu (directional with Cmd+Shift+Arrows)
- Resize Split submenu (equalize, move dividers)
- Dynamic tab list at bottom of menu
- Added submenu CSS styling

fix(mux): merge dialogs, add rsync options, fix show-all-tabs menu

- Combine download + folder sync into single download dialog with rsync options
- Add rsync options (exclude, delete, preview) to upload dialog
- Remove separate folder-transfer dialog
- Fix show-all-tabs menu item not working
fix(mux): add primary button style for download dialog

fix(mux): make debug stats overlay a singleton with transparent bg

- Only display one global stats overlay instead of per-panel
- Use fixed position attached to body
- More transparent background (0.5 instead of 0.8)

fix(mux): restore inspector UI and fix binary protocol

- Restore original inspector UI with left/right panels, tabs, resize
- Fix binary inspector_subscribe to send proper 6-byte message
- Server sends initial inspector_state after subscribe
- Fix upload/download dialogs (focus input, enter/esc handlers)
- Remove debug timing spam from backend

fix(download): show save dialog first, handle viewport changes

- Show save dialog immediately when download begins (before download)
- Track save paths per download GUID for concurrent downloads
- Use actual frame dimensions from screencast metadata
- Update coordinate mapper when Chrome viewport changes (download bar)
- Add click stall detection for quick screenshot recovery
- Clean up temp files on completion or cancel

fix(viewer): handle SPA navigation screencast stall with screenshot fallback

Chrome's PIPE-based screencast stops sending frames after SPA navigation.
This adds immediate screenshot capture on SPA nav to show updated content.

Changes:
- Add needs_screencast_reset flag triggered by Page.navigatedWithinDocument
- Take screenshot immediately after SPA navigation (100ms delay for render)
- Remove white background injection from captureScreenshot (was causing side effects)
- Add session refresh on reset (re-attach to target)
- Send screencast commands through both PIPE and WebSocket as fallback
- Add running check in handleResponse to prevent shutdown race condition
- Add frame pool reset function for screencast restart

perf(encoder): use high quality vImage scaling, cap FPS at 30

- Switch to kvImageHighQualityResampling for better image quality when
  downscaling to 1080p
- Cap termweb max FPS at 30 (was 60) to match encoder output capability
- Keep mux server render at 30fps, input polling at 125Hz for responsiveness
- Simplify encoder by removing failed threading experiments

perf(mux): optimize video encoding with vImage scaling

- Use Accelerate framework vImage for hardware-accelerated downscaling
  (24ms -> 9ms, 2.5x faster)
- Remove frame change detection skip - send all frames, let encoder
  handle redundancy with P-frames
- Add detailed encode timing breakdown (scale/pixbuf/submit/complete)
- Link Accelerate framework in build.zig

Combined with previous max_pixels scaling, encode time dropped from
37ms to ~21ms, achieving 26-27 FPS (up from 11-12 FPS).

feat(mux): add max_pixels downscaling for video encoder

- Scale down high-resolution captures to ~1080p (MAX_PIXELS = 1920*1080)
- Keeps aspect ratio when downscaling
- Adds hardware encoder preference flag
- Adds debug timing output for render loop profiling
- Fixes quality level thresholds for adaptive bitrate

3600x1948 (7MP) now scales to ~1958x1060 (2MP) before H.264 encoding,
reducing encode time significantly.

fix(viewer): add placement_id to prevent Kitty graphics accumulation

Without a fixed placement_id, each frame created a NEW placement of the
same image, causing Ghostty to accumulate textures in VRAM. This led to
texture thrashing where ghostty_surface_draw degraded from 2ms to 500ms+
as VRAM filled up and macOS started swapping.

Using both fixed image_id=100 AND placement_id=100 tells Kitty protocol
to replace the existing placement instead of accumulating new ones.

feat: add `termweb mux` command to main CLI

- Build termweb-mux alongside termweb from root build.zig (macOS only)
- Add `mux` subcommand that execs termweb-mux binary
- Refactor mux main.zig to expose run() function for future direct integration
- Users can now run `termweb mux` instead of separate termweb-mux binary

perf: faster new tab by lazy video encoder init

Client:
- Show "Connecting..." immediately on canvas while waiting for first frame

Server:
- Defer VideoEncoder and bgra_buffer allocation to first frame capture
- Panel creation no longer blocks on VideoToolbox session creation
- Reduces time from click to visible terminal

fix(web): correct mouse protocol from TS migration

- Use Float64 (8 bytes) for coordinates, not Float32 (4 bytes)
- Fix buffer sizes: MOUSE_MOVE=18, MOUSE_INPUT=20, MOUSE_SCROLL=34
- Remove devicePixelRatio multiplication - server expects CSS pixels

feat(web): add iOS accessory drawer with sticky modifiers

- Add collapsible drawer at bottom for touch-only devices
- Keys: Esc, Tab, Ctrl, Alt, Cmd (left) and arrows (right)
- Sticky modifiers work with both accessory keys and keyboard input
- CSS media query (hover: none, pointer: coarse) for touch detection
- Fix WebCodecs decoder to wait for keyframe before decoding
- Fix debug overlay to work with both #debug and ?debug params

fix(web): restore mobile menu handling lost in TS migration

Add back hamburger toggle, menu-label click handlers, and close-on-
click-outside behavior that was present in original client.js but
missing from the TypeScript migration.

perf(web): switch to WebCodecs for lower latency H.264 decoding

Replace jMuxer/MSE with WebCodecs VideoDecoder for direct H.264 to
canvas rendering. This eliminates the fMP4 muxing and MSE buffering
overhead for lower end-to-end latency.

- Decode H.264 NAL units directly via VideoDecoder
- Render decoded VideoFrame to canvas with drawImage()
- Parse Annex B format and extract codec from SPS
- Add debug stats overlay (enable with #debug=1 in URL)
  - Shows render FPS, received FPS, decode queue, latency, health

feat(mux): switch to H.264 video encoding with MSE playback

- Add VideoToolbox H.264 encoder for hardware-accelerated encoding
- Use jMuxer + MSE for client-side video playback via <video> element
- Add adaptive bitrate based on client buffer health reporting
- Remove old WebGPU/canvas rendering and zlib compression code
- Clean up unused client.ts and worker.ts files
- Fix input handling to match original protocol format

This significantly reduces CPU usage by leveraging hardware video
encode/decode instead of software compression and WebGPU rendering.

fix(mux): pass pixel dimensions to ghostty for proper retina rendering

ghostty_surface_set_size expects pixel dimensions, not CSS points.
This fixes the 1/4 canvas rendering issue on HiDPI displays.

perf(mux): add adaptive FPS for lower CPU usage

- 15 FPS when idle (cursor blink) vs 60 FPS when typing
- Throttle before computing diff to let changes accumulate
- Remove debug timing output

perf(mux): optimize frame processing - reduce CPU from 50% to 20%

Key optimizations:
- Add partial_delta frame type: skip compression for small changes (<500KB)
- Use SIMD std.mem.eql for 4KB block comparisons
- IOSurface seed check to skip unchanged surfaces
- Single memcpy when strides match
- Keyframe interval increased to 60 seconds

For cursor blink: sends 243KB uncompressed instead of compressing 14MB
perf(mux): increase keyframe interval to 60 seconds

perf(mux): zero-copy frame diff for reduced CPU during cursor blink

Fuse BGRA comparison, RGB conversion, and XOR diff in single pass.
Only write to diff buffer and update rgb_previous where pixels changed.
Track dirty regions to minimize clearing between frames.

For cursor blink: ~200 bytes written instead of 42MB per frame.

fix(server): use atomic flag to prevent duplicate shutdown messages

First Ctrl+C triggers graceful shutdown, second Ctrl+C forces
immediate exit. Uses atomic swap to ensure each case only executes once.

fix(server): add SIGINT handler for graceful Ctrl+C shutdown

Server now properly handles Ctrl+C by setting running flag to false,
allowing clean shutdown instead of requiring kill -9.

feat(mux): add desktop notification support via OSC 9

- Add panel_notification binary message type (0x0D)
- Handle GHOSTTY_ACTION_DESKTOP_NOTIFICATION in action callback
- Broadcast notifications to web clients with title and body
- Show browser Notification API popup when permission granted
- Add visual indicator on background tabs for notifications
- Click notification to focus window and switch to source panel
- Auto-close notifications after 5 seconds

Also includes compression level optimization for frame encoding.

fix(server): return 204 for /favicon.ico requests

Browser automatically requests /favicon.ico regardless of inline SVG
favicon in HTML. Return 204 No Content to suppress 404 errors.

fix(web): prevent layout_update from clearing existing tabs

- Fix handleLayoutUpdate to ignore server layout updates (matching
  original JS behavior) instead of calling restoreLayoutFromServer
  which was destroying all existing tabs
- Fix updatePanelPwd to update tab indicator when pwd changes
- Fix updateIndicatorForPanel to take title argument and update both
  title-indicator and title-state-indicator elements
- Fix updateTitleIndicator to accept folder and state indicators
- Fix addTabUI HTML to match original (single line, no whitespace)
- Add missing quickTerminalPanel and previousActivePanel state
- Add destroy() method for proper cleanup on page unload
- Add reconnection guard to prevent actions after destroy
fix(web): build as IIFE for browser compatibility
build(mux): add Makefile for web + zig build
feat(web): add build:all script for TS + Zig

chore(web): gitignore client.js build artifact

Source is now TypeScript, client.js is build output.
Run `bun run build` to generate.

feat(web): complete TypeScript migration with all features

Add full App class with:
- Tab management with history (LRU switching)
- Split panel support
- Command palette with keyboard shortcuts
- Upload/download dialogs
- Access control dialog for auth management
- Tab overview with live previews
- Binary protocol handlers for server messages

New modules:
- dialogs.ts: CommandPalette, UploadDialog, DownloadDialog, AccessControlDialog
- file-transfer.ts: FileTransferHandler with compression

Build output: 63.23 KB minified (8 modules)

feat(web): add Panel and SplitContainer TypeScript modules

- Extract Panel class to panel.ts with full WebGPU rendering
- Add SplitContainer class for pane splitting
- Add color utilities (hexToRgb, luminance, shadow/highlight)
- Update applyColors to derive all colors from config
- Add reparent method to Panel for container changes

feat(web): add TypeScript setup with Bun

- package.json with bun build/dev/typecheck scripts
- tsconfig.json with WebGPU types
- Protocol constants and types in protocol.ts
- Type definitions in types.ts
- Utility functions in utils.ts
- Main app structure in index.ts (minimal implementation)

Original client.js kept working, new TS code builds to dist/client.js
Run: bun install && bun run build

feat: add folder download as zip using libdeflate

- New zip.zig module creates ZIP files using libdeflate for compression
- Reuses existing libdeflate (no new dependencies, just added crc32.c)
- Server handler: 0x14 folder_download -> 0x15 folder.zip response
- Client auto-detects folder (path ending with /) vs file download
- ZIP format: local headers + DEFLATE data + central directory

fix: remove double compression for file downloads

Single file downloads were being compressed twice:
1. Server: libdeflate compression
2. WebSocket: permessage-deflate

This caused pako decompression to fail on libdeflate output.
Now send uncompressed data and let permessage-deflate handle it.

feat(sdk): add Tauri-style invoke, events, channels, and fs APIs

New SDK features inspired by Tauri:
- invoke() with return values and command() for backend handlers
- emit()/listen() bidirectional event system
- channel() for streaming data
- window API (setSize, navigate, back, forward, reload)
- fs API (readFile, writeFile, readDir, stat, mkdir, remove)
- TypeScript definitions
- Browser-side SDK for page integration

perf: use socket timeout instead of polling for WebSocket I/O

- Add setReadTimeout() to use SO_RCVTIMEO instead of Thread.sleep polling
- Reads return immediately when data arrives, timeout only for shutdown check
- Add event_loop.zig with kqueue (macOS) and epoll (Linux) support
- Fix auth token generation: 32→30 bytes to fit base64 in 44-byte token

perf: reduce CPU usage with adaptive sleep intervals

- WebSocket idle sleep: 1ms → 5ms when no data
- Render loop: sleep 33ms when idle, 8ms only when streaming/input
- Add hasQueuedInput() for quick non-locking check

feat(dashboard): add io_uring and dispatch_apply for faster process stats

Optimize dashboard process list collection:
- Linux: Use io_uring to batch /proc file reads (2-3x faster)
- macOS: Use dispatch_apply for parallel proc_pidinfo calls (2-4x faster)
- Fallback to sequential reads if io_uring unavailable

feat: add session and access control system

- First login is admin with no auth required
- Admin can set up password or passkey authentication
- Role-based access: admin, editor (write), viewer (read-only)
- Share links with editor/viewer tokens
- Sessions with separate terminal tabs per session
- Binary protocol for auth messages (0x90-0x9F)
- Access Control dialog in admin menu
- Tokens stored in ~/.termweb/auth.json

feat: add fast file transfer with io_uring, dispatch_io, and XXH3

Implement high-performance file transfer system faster than rsync:
- Binary WebSocket protocol on port 8082 for file transfers
- io_uring on Linux for async pipelined I/O
- dispatch_io on macOS for async I/O with GCD
- XXH3 SIMD-accelerated hashing (3-5x faster than xxHash64)
- Parallel file hashing and chunk compression using dispatch
- mmap for zero-copy file reads
- libdeflate compression with resume support
- Folder sync dialog with exclude patterns and dry-run preview

fix(freeze): fix shard init buffer size and stub CoW allocator

- Increase init_buf from 128 to 256 bytes to fit the full init header
  (the header is ~180 bytes, was silently failing with catch continue)
- Stub cow_allocator.zig since prebuilt WAMR lacks custom callbacks

feat(freeze): implement sharded compilation for large frozen modules

This solves the LLVM "TU explosion" issue where compiling 138MB+ of
frozen Zig code crashes LLVM. The solution:

1. Each frozen shard (frozen_shard_N.zig) now exports its own
   `frozen_init_shard_N()` function that registers its functions

2. The main frozen_module.zig uses `extern fn` declarations to call
   shard init functions, avoiding @import which creates one giant TU

3. build.zig compiles each shard as a separate object file with
   ReleaseSmall optimization, then links them to the executable

Architecture:
- Generated code: Each shard ~200KB, compiled independently by LLVM
- 735 shards for TSC = 735 parallel compilation jobs
- Main module is thin (~5KB), just calls shard init functions

Build time estimate:
- Before: LLVM crash after 1-1.5 hours
- After: ~45 seconds to 2 minutes (parallel compilation)

Affected targets:
- native-static
- native (embed)
- wasm-static
- wasm-standalone

fix(freeze): reduce frozen code size for large codebases

- Set DISPATCH_TABLE_THRESHOLD to 10000 (use comptime switch instead of
  runtime dispatch tables for most functions)
- This reduces generated code size by ~37% (217MB -> 138MB for TSC)
- Add frozen_optimize_arg with ReleaseSafe to reduce LLVM memory pressure
- Increase bytecode read limit to 1GB for large bundles

Note: This reduces but does not eliminate LLVM compilation issues for
very large frozen modules. A proper fix requires sharded compilation
where each shard is compiled as a separate object file.

fix(freeze): fix isConstructorCall and add debug checks for var_ref

- Pass ctx parameter to isConstructorCall for proper JS_IsFunction check
- Add shared cleanup helpers for locals and arg_shadow in opcode_emitter
- Add debug checks in QuickJS patch for corrupted objects in var_ref

refactor(mux): hide mux binary, use termweb mux subcommand

- Rename termweb-mux to .termweb-mux (hidden binary)
- Update cli.zig to find hidden binary
- Users only see 'termweb' binary, run mux via 'termweb mux'

docs: focus README on terminal multiplexer

- Rewrite root README to highlight termweb mux as main feature
- Use subcommand style (termweb mux) instead of separate binary
- Keep web browser (termweb open) as secondary feature

docs(mux): update README for cross-platform support

Add Linux VA-API hardware encoding documentation and update
web client code for improved connection handling.

feat(mux): scale-to-zero and server-authoritative tabs

- Lazy initialize ghostty on first panel creation, free when last closes
- Make app/config optional, add ensureGhosttyInit/freeGhosttyIfEmpty
- Frontend follows server state - no auto-create tabs
- Reset title to 👻 when all tabs are closed
- HTTP server accepts optional ghostty config
- Add WebSocket upgrade routing by path (/ws/panel, /ws/control, /ws/file)

fix(mux): prevent NOTSOCK panic when closing WebSocket

Add is_open check in sendClose() to avoid writing to already-closed
sockets, which caused unreachable NOTSOCK panics during shutdown.

fix(mux): server-authoritative tabs, fix build and memory leak

Frontend changes:
- Remove auto-create tab on empty panel_list (server is authoritative)
- Frontend stays empty when server has no panels, user creates manually

Build changes:
- Touch assets.zig after JS build to force zig to re-embed
- Remove duplicate bun build from build.zig (Makefile handles it)

Memory leak fix:
- Free selection_clipboard in Server.deinit()

fix(mux): wait for ghostty to render before reading pixels on Linux

Add ticks_since_connect counter to track frames since panel connection.
Wait 3 frames (~100ms) before attempting to read pixels from ghostty's
OpenGL framebuffer, giving it time to render initial content. This fixes
blank panel on first connection requiring a page reload on Linux.

fix(mux): platform-specific keycodes and graceful shutdown

- Add XKB keycodes for Linux, keep macOS virtual keycodes
  (fixes backspace and other keys not working on Linux)
- Track active WebSocket connections with atomic counter
- Wait for connection threads to finish before destroying server
- Fix shutdown order: close WebSocket servers before destroying panels
  to prevent use-after-free crashes during disconnect callbacks
chore(mux): remove WebSocket debug logging

fix(mux): make initial title Linux-only

macOS already has working title updates via auto-injected shell integration

fix(mux): clean up tab title initialization

- Remove initial_input that typed visible commands into terminal
- Fix hostname buffer scope issue (was causing undefined behavior)
- Simplify initial title to "user@hostname:~"
- Remove debug logging
- Keep only GHOSTTY_SHELL_FEATURES env var for shell integration

fix(mux): enable Linux tab title updates

- Set GHOSTTY_SHELL_FEATURES env var to enable shell integration features
- Configure PROMPT_COMMAND via initial_input to send title escape sequences
- Send initial title (user@host:home) when panel is created
- Add debug logging for SET_TITLE and PWD actions on Linux

The title updates now work via:
1. Initial title sent when panel is created
2. PROMPT_COMMAND configured to send OSC 0 title on each prompt
3. Shell integration features enabled via environment variable

chore(mux): add missing cross-platform video encoder abstraction

Add video.zig which provides a cross-platform interface that selects
the appropriate encoder at compile time:
- macOS: VideoToolbox hardware encoder
- Linux: VA-API hardware encoder

Also add ghostty_stub.zig with type stubs for Linux compilation.

fix(mux): implement Linux VA-API H.264 video encoding

- Add video_encoder_linux.zig with VA-API hardware encoding
- Use H264Main profile for better driver compatibility
- Use vaCreateImage + vaPutImage for pixel upload (more reliable)
- Upload to src_surface and encode from it
- Generate matching SPS/PPS NAL units for Main profile
- Rebuild libghostty.a with EGL headless pixel reading fixes

feat(ghostty): add Linux headless EGL rendering support

Update ghostty vendor with headless EGL rendering for Linux:

- Add egl_headless.zig for dynamic EGL/GBM loading
- Support GBM platform for hardware GPU rendering
- Handle threadlocal glad context with must_draw_from_app_thread
- Rebuild libghostty.a with Linux headless support

This enables termweb-mux to run with real GPU rendering on Linux
servers without a display, using DRM render nodes for acceleration.

feat(mux): add Linux EGL headless support for libghostty

- Add ghostty as git submodule
- Add patch for Linux EGL headless rendering (OpenGL 4.3)
- Include prebuilt libghostty.a for Linux
- Update Makefile with vendor-reset using git reset --hard

feat(simd): add cross-platform shared memory for IPC

Add shared memory abstraction using comptime for platform selection:
- Linux: memfd_create + mmap for anonymous shared memory
- macOS: MAP_ANONYMOUS for same-process use
- Fallback: temp file + mmap for other platforms

Includes SharedFramePool for zero-copy frame transfer between
processes (e.g., termweb <-> mux server) with lock-free ring buffer.

Also update Makefile to build mux web client before zig build,
ensuring bundled JS is available for embedding.
chore(mux): gitignore vendor directory

fix(tree-shake): detect TypeScript bundles via createSourceFile

The previous trigger (__edgebox_intercept_tsc_factory) was defined BY
tsc.js, not used by TypeScript bundles, so tsc.js was never included.

Now triggers on createSourceFile which appears 10+ times in TypeScript
bundles, enabling the native node registration hooks.

Verified: tsc.js now appears in runtime globals when compiling TSC.

feat(freeze): wire native shapes codegen for relooper path

Add native shape property access optimization to opcode_emitter.zig,
which is used by the relooper codegen for functions with complex
control flow. Properties like kind, flags, pos, end, parent, and
cached atom properties (symbol, escapedName, etc.) now emit calls
to zig_runtime.nativeGet* helpers.

that was causing a linker error.

to activate the speedup. Currently falls back to slow path.

perf(freeze): add frozen_impl cache and wyhash for dispatch

1. Add PointerHashMap to hashmap_helper using wyhash for bytecode lookups
2. Switch bytecode_registry to use PointerHashMap (consistent with name_registry)
3. Add frozen_impl field to JSFunctionBytecode for direct pointer caching
4. Cache frozen function pointer after first lookup - subsequent calls
   skip hashmap entirely and call the cached pointer directly

The cache eliminates hashmap lookup overhead on repeated function calls.
First call: hashmap lookup + cache
Subsequent calls: direct pointer call (no hashmap)

fix(freeze): add NULL check in free_var_ref and auto-derive binary name

- Add NULL prev pointer check in free_var_ref to handle frozen/static
  var_refs that were never added to the GC list
- Auto-derive output binary name from source file (e.g., _tsc.js -> _tsc)
  when -Doutput is not specified

frozen closures that bypass the normal GC list registration.

fix(freeze): fix sharded compilation linking and arena allocator safety

- Use extern fn declarations in shards instead of importing native_dispatch
  to prevent duplicate symbol errors when compiling shards separately
- Add isArenaPointer() guard in arena allocator to safely ignore
  frozen/static pointers that don't belong to the arena
- Tighten LIFO optimization bounds check with HEADER_SIZE offset to
  prevent integer underflow on pointers near block start
- Remove export keyword from frozen_reset_call_depth_zig to avoid
  duplicate symbol when zig_runtime is imported by multiple shards

This fixes LLVM TU explosion by allowing 738 shards to compile as
separate object files (~200KB each) instead of one 138MB+ module.

fix(quickjs): defer var_ref freeing in gc_free_cycles to prevent use-after-free

The GC cycle freeing code was freeing var_refs directly, but functions
in the same cycle would later try to access those var_refs in their
finalizers, causing use-after-free.

Fix:
1. Free var_ref's captured value immediately (while objects are valid)
2. Set ref_count=0 so free_var_ref() skips already-freed var_refs
3. Move var_ref to deferred list instead of freeing immediately
4. Free var_ref structs after all finalizers have run

fix(freeze): fix set_loc sp accounting and countStackRefs deduplication

Three related fixes for stack pointer (sp) misalignment in frozen codegen:

1. set_loc base stack laundering (zig_codegen_full.zig): When set_loc
   vpop'd a base stack reference ("stack[sp-N]"), it replaced it with
   "locals[N]" in the vstack, losing the stack position info. This caused
   materializeVStack to miscalculate sp adjustments (+1 instead of +0),
   leaving orphan values on the stack. Fix: push the stack reference back
   when the vpop'd expression is a stack ref.

2. countStackRefs deduplication (zig_codegen_relooper.zig): The relooper's
   countStackRefs counted ALL occurrences of "stack[sp" instead of unique
   offsets. Expressions like is_undefined_or_null that reference the same
   stack position twice got sp decremented by 2 instead of 1. Fix: port
   the deduplication logic from zig_codegen_full.zig.

3. is_undefined_or_null vpop/vpush (opcode_emitter.zig): Changed to use
   vpop/vpush pattern for correct stack tracking.

4. Removed NOT_FUNC debug traces from quickjs.c patch.

These fixes resolve the "TypeError: not a function" crash in frozen TSC
compilation where nodeModuleNameResolverWorker's 9-arg call read the
function from the wrong stack position (off by 2).

fix(freeze): add exception propagation for function calls

The frozen codegen was generating call code without exception checks,
causing the interpreter to continue with exception values instead of
propagating them. This led to frozen functions appearing to hang when
they should have failed fast.

- Add isException() checks after JSValue.call in both codegens
- Fix in zig_codegen_full.zig (call, call0, call_method)
- Fix in zig_codegen_relooper.zig (call, call_method, apply)
- Make getAtomByIndex public in module_parser (needed by frozen_registry)
- Add getHeapBase() to native_arena for pointer compression

This makes frozen functions properly propagate exceptions, revealing
the actual underlying errors instead of appearing to hang.

feat(mux): replace fzstd npm package with Zig WASM zstd module

Build a Zig WASM module (wasm32-wasi) that compiles vendored zstd C
sources for browser-side compress + decompress, replacing the JS-only
fzstd package (which only supported decompression).

- Add packages/mux/wasm/zstd_wasm.zig exporting zstd_alloc, zstd_free,
  zstd_compress, zstd_decompress, zstd_compress_bound, zstd_frame_content_size
- Add WASM build target in build.zig (zig build wasm)
- Embed zstd.wasm in server binary via assets.zig, serve at /zstd.wasm
- Rewrite browser zstd-wasm.ts to load Zig WASM with WASI stubs
- Simplify compression-pool.ts and file-transfer.ts (remove deflate fallback)
- Remove fzstd dependency, collectStreamChunks, unused exports

refactor(mux): add PanelKind enum, remove dead file handlers and libdeflate

- Add PanelKind enum (regular, quick_terminal) to Panel struct, replacing
  the is_quick_terminal bool flag with proper type system support
- Remove ~500 lines of dead code: old file upload/download/preview handlers
  on control WS (client uses file WS transfer protocol with zstd)
- Remove ControlMsgType enum (dead JSON legacy)
- Remove dead BinaryCtrlMsg entries (0x84-0x87 split/inspector on control WS)
- Remove libdeflate.h includes from main.zig, terminal.zig, platform.zig
  (only zip.zig still needs libdeflate for ZIP format compliance)
- Fix file transfer compression mismatch: server now detects zstd by magic
  bytes, accepts raw data when browser has no zstd compressor

refactor(mux): binary-only protocol, active tab streaming, single source of truth

- Convert inspector messages from JSON to binary on panel WS (0x30-0x32)
- Remove all JSON control message handlers and parsers
- Remove dead code: InspectorSubscription, onViewAction, deprecated file handlers
- Only encode+send H.264 frames for panels in active tab (saves VA-API time)
- Force keyframes on tab switch for clean decoder recovery
- Replace active_tab_id + per-tab active_panel_id with single layout.active_panel_id
- Derive active tab from active panel (getActiveTabId)
- Client setActivePanel derives tab, sends FOCUS_PANEL to server

fix(mux): fix right-side corruption on new panels by setting viewport before each draw

OpenGL.surfaceSize() reads GL_VIEWPORT to determine surface dimensions,
but in headless multi-surface mode all surfaces share one GL context.
When a half-width panel drew last, the viewport leaked to the next
full-width panel, causing surfaceSize() to return the wrong dimensions.
The renderer only drew the left portion, leaving the right side with
stale/garbage data that compressed to 2.7MB H.264 frames.

Fix: set GL viewport to the surface's pixel dimensions in
embedded.zig draw() before each core_surface.draw() call.

fix(mux): fix render loop mutex starvation causing 5+ second new panel delay

The render loop held self.mutex for the entire frame capture+encode+send
loop (120-550ms per iteration depending on panel count). This starved the
WebSocket handler thread trying to queue split/create requests, causing
3.9+ second delays before new panels could be created.

Fix: collect panel pointers into a local buffer under the mutex, then
release it before doing the heavy work (readPixels, VA-API encode,
WebSocket send). Mutex hold time drops from 120-550ms to <1ms.

Also includes:
- SharedVaContext: share VA-API display/config across encoders for fast
  panel init (~9ms vs ~400ms)
- Rename calcScaledDimensions to calcAlignedDimensions, bump level_idc
  to 52 for large frame support
- Add diagnostic timing instrumentation for ongoing corruption investigation

fix(mux): raise encoder MAX_PIXELS to 4K and revert pipeline

Reverts the pipelined encoder which caused text quality issues and slow
page loads. Raises MAX_PIXELS from 1920x1080 to 4096x2304 (H.264 Level
5.1) so full-resolution encoding covers all common displays without
downscaling. Bumps MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE to 4MB accordingly.

perf(mux): implement pipelined video encoder to eliminate GPU stalls

Pipeline encoding so frame N-1's output is read while frame N is submitted,
avoiding blocking vaSyncSurface calls. Also adds nearest-neighbor scaling
when source dimensions exceed encoder limits, and uses glFlush instead of
glFinish for non-blocking GL synchronization.

fix(mux): fix HTTP server shutdown requiring double Ctrl+C

- Add stopped flag to prevent double-stop race condition
- Close listener socket in stop() to interrupt accept() immediately
- Check running flag after accept errors to exit cleanly

fix(mux): use surface pointer for PBO state to fix multi-panel isolation

- Change PBO state manager to key by surface_id (pointer) instead of
  fbo_id, preventing state collisions when OpenGL recycles FBO IDs
- Add strict GL state isolation (PACK_ALIGNMENT, PACK_ROW_LENGTH,
  PACK_SKIP_PIXELS, PACK_SKIP_ROWS) before every pixel read
- Add glFinish() synchronization to prevent GPU race conditions
- Force initial render on panel creation to populate FBO immediately
- Add encodeWithDimensions() for explicit frame dimension handling

fix(freeze): fix materializeVStack cross-expression dedup and switch pattern sp

Three related sp accounting fixes:

1. materializeVStack: Use countStackRefsMulti to deduplicate stack refs
   ACROSS all vstack items, not just within each one. When two vstack
   items both reference stack[sp-1], they consume 1 unique stack
   position, not 2.

2. Switch pattern (relooper): Don't pop the discriminant in the emitted
   switch statement. The original if-else chain (dup+push+eq+if_false)
   has net-0 sp change, so case handler blocks and default continuation
   blocks expect the discriminant still on the stack.

3. Add sp overflow/underflow detection at block entries for debugging.
   Emits checks in both full codegen and relooper paths.

fix: quit button now exits cleanly without requiring Ctrl+C

The input thread was blocking on a synchronous stdin read(), so
thread.join() in stopInputThread would hang until new input arrived.
Fix by setting stdin to O_NONBLOCK before joining, which causes
the blocked read to return WouldBlock and lets the thread exit.

fix: auto-save downloads to ~/Downloads/ on Linux instead of silent deletion

On Linux, the GUI file picker (zenity/kdialog) fails silently in
headless/SSH sessions, causing downloads to be saved to a temp file
and immediately deleted. Fix by:

- Adding handleDownloadWillBeginAsync to register downloads without
  blocking on a GUI picker (Linux only)
- Falling back to ~/Downloads/{filename} when no save path is set
- Showing a brief "Saved: {path}" notification in the toolbar for 3s
- macOS behavior unchanged (native osascript picker still used)

fix(mux): use trailing-edge throttle for mouse move to reduce cursor lag

The previous leading-edge-only throttle fired on the first event then
dropped all subsequent positions for 33ms, causing the cursor to stick
at stale positions during fast movement. Now the latest position is
also sent at the end of each throttle window, so the cursor always
catches up to where the mouse actually is.

perf(mux): replace Canvas 2D drawImage with WebGPU zero-copy rendering

Use importExternalTexture to bind decoded VideoFrame GPU textures
directly as shader inputs, eliminating the pixel copy from drawImage.
Falls back to Canvas 2D when WebGPU is unavailable.

perf(mux): add per-frame spike and gap detection to perf log

Log SPIKE lines immediately when any single frame exceeds 50ms with
per-phase breakdown (tick/read/enc/keyframe/send_fail). Log GAP lines
when frame-to-frame interval exceeds 100ms to catch sleep overshoots
or pre-frame work stalls.

fix(mux): prevent browser hangs from silent frame drops and decoder error leaks

- Propagate WouldBlock from sendFrame instead of silently swallowing it,
  force keyframe after dropped frames so browser H.264 decoder can recover
- Track frames_dropped in perf log for visibility into send failures
- Fix VideoDecoder error callback to reset pendingDecode and recover the
  decoder instead of leaking pending count permanently
- Use decoder.decodeQueueSize for P-frame drop decisions (accurate,
  browser-tracked) instead of manually tracked pendingDecode counter
- Prefer hardware H.264 decode to reduce CPU load at high resolutions

fix(mux): prevent WebSocket protocol corruption and improve reliability

- Fix partial frame corruption: if writev writes partial data then gets
  WouldBlock, close connection instead of leaving corrupt frame in TCP
  buffer. Only drop frames when nothing has been written yet.
- Fix incomplete frame read: return BrokenPipe on short reads instead of
  processing partial WebSocket frames as complete messages.
- Add per-server write timeout: panel/preview sockets use 10ms (frames
  are droppable), control/file keep 1s (messages must not be lost).
- Revert sendFrame to hold mutex during send to prevent use-after-free
  (safe now that sends are bounded to 10ms max).

fix(mux): reduce mutex contention and fix data races in render loop

- sendFrame(): snapshot connection under lock, send outside to avoid
  blocking input handlers during WebSocket I/O
- sendPreviewFrame(): snapshot connections under mutex to prevent
  iterator invalidation race with disconnect handlers
- processPendingDestroys(): move sendClose() outside mutex to avoid
  blocking render loop during TCP I/O

fix(mux): non-blocking WebSocket sends and remove harmful optimizations

The root cause of typing lag variance (10ms to 10s) was writev spin-looping
on WouldBlock when the TCP send buffer was full. This blocked the entire
render loop — no frames, no input processing — until the browser drained
the buffer.

Fix: return WouldBlock instead of spinning, dropping the frame. The next
frame will contain updated content. Also removed dirty frame detection and
immediate-render optimizations that were adding complexity without helping.

perf(mux): add burst FPS, immediate rendering, and server-side focus for new panels

- Add render_immediately atomic flag to bypass frame timing on input/focus
- Burst FPS: temporarily increase to 60fps for ~1.5s after activity, then 30fps idle
- splitPanel() now sets active_panel_id so new splits are focused server-side
- processPendingPanels/Splits trigger render_immediately for instant focus feedback

feat(mux): add cursor blinking, focus management, and UI improvements

- Add app-thread cursor blink timer in embedded.zig (renderer thread's
  timer cannot drive rendering in headless mode)
- Sync renderer focus state directly on app thread, bypassing unreliable
  mailbox delivery
- Track focus transitions to avoid per-frame set_focus overhead
- Unfocused panels show solid block cursor instead of hollow
- Shorten cursor sprites by 1px top/bottom to fix bottom-row clipping
- Set termweb defaults: cursor-style=bar, cursor-style-blink=true
- Skip both updateFrame and drawFrame on renderer thread when
  must_draw_from_app_thread is true
- Remove non-functional split buttons from empty state page
- Make command palette clickable on empty state page
- Rename "No Open Tabs" to "Welcome to Termweb"

fix(mux): fix renderer data race, signal handler deadlock, and UI title updates

- Move updateFrame() to app thread in Surface.draw() when
  must_draw_from_app_thread is true, eliminating the data race between
  renderer thread's updateFrame and app thread's drawFrame that caused
  heap corruption (double free). The renderer thread now skips
  updateFrame entirely for this case.

- Make SIGINT handler signal-safe by removing .stop() calls and
  std.debug.print (both use allocators that can deadlock). Server
  shutdown is now done after the render loop exits. Fixes double
  Ctrl+C required to quit.

- Add SIGABRT handler with backtrace and crash phase tracking for
  diagnosing any remaining crash issues.

- Fix clipboard callback to complete requests properly.

- Update LICENSE from Apache-2.0 to MIT to match package.json/README.

- Add dynamic titlebar and document.title that follow the active tab's
  terminal title (matching ghostty's single set_title model).

- Change HTML title from ghost emoji to "termweb" to avoid duplicate
  ghost with favicon.

fix(mux): fix new tab stuck on loading, add raw mode prompt, add shutdown debug

- Fix race condition where first keyframe was lost because WebGPU renderer
  wasn't ready yet: decoder now starts immediately and caches the latest
  VideoFrame until the renderer is initialized, then flushes it
- Connection picker prompt now uses raw terminal mode for single-keypress
  selection (no Enter needed), with fallback for piped stdin
- Add shutdown debug prints to trace which server stop step hangs

feat(mux): add multiplayer pane assignment, adaptive frame rate, and disable server cursor blink

- Multiplayer: admin can assign panels to sessions, coworkers send input
  via control WS with session validation, broadcast assignments/client list
- Disable server-side cursor blink (cursor-style-blink=false) to eliminate
  idle H.264 frames when terminal is not changing
- Adaptive frame rate: skip draw+readpixels when consecutive_unchanged
  exceeds idle threshold, reducing CPU/GPU usage during idle
- Frontend: require WebGPU (remove 2D canvas fallback), defer decoder init
  until renderer ready, add coworker input routing via sendInput()
- Ghostty patch: add ghostty_surface_cursor_info C API for cursor state
- Debug: add /tmp/termweb-panel-debug.log for tracing frame delivery
- Fix tunnel URL wait (signal url_ready on pipe close)
- Forward auth token from page URL to WebSocket connections
fix: remove unused shortcut for toggling inspector in README

fix(mux): fix tunnel URL display for tailscale/ngrok and show errors

- Tailscale: pipe stderr (not stdout) since tailscale funnel outputs
  there; add fallback to `tailscale funnel status` for existing configs
- ngrok: handle optional spaces in JSON URL parsing
- All providers: display error messages instead of failing silently
  (ngrok auth errors, tailscale config issues, cloudflare errors)
- Fix tunnel.zig syntax errors (missing ngrok switch cases)
- Kill tunnel subprocess with SIGKILL on SIGINT for clean shutdown
- CLI: use --cloudflare/--ngrok/--tailscale instead of --expose

feat(mux): add tunnel sharing, share dialog, and frame skip alignment

- Add tunnel provider integration (cloudflare, ngrok, tailscale) with
  interactive picker on startup and CLI flags (--cloudflare, --ngrok,
  --tailscale, --local)
- Display QR code and LAN URL in terminal for easy mobile access
- Replace admin/auth menu with Share dialog showing URL + QR code
- Add main client election so only one browser tab controls the terminal
- Align frame skip between macOS (IOSurface seed) and Linux (XxHash64)
- Fix macOS frame skip bug where captureFromIOSurface return was ignored
- Set keyframe interval to 600 frames (20s) on both platforms

fix(mux): fix Cmd+Shift+V hotkey and remove unused undo/redo

- Move paste_from_selection handler inside the Cmd+Shift catch-all block
  so it executes before falling through to split selection arrow checks
- Add Cmd+C/V/A keyboard shortcuts routed through handleCommand so they
  use VIEW_ACTION instead of raw key events (needed for paste clipboard)
- Remove undo/redo from Edit menu and command palette (no-op in terminal)

fix(mux): fix VIEW_ACTION protocol mismatch, add clipboard paste, and improve shortcut fonts

- Fix sendViewAction encoding action_len as u16 instead of u8, causing
  all menu/palette actions to arrive with a leading null byte (broken)
- Fix Edit menu action strings to match Ghostty binding names
  (copy_to_clipboard, paste_from_clipboard, paste_from_selection, select_all)
- Add SET_CLIPBOARD (0x8C) control message so browser clipboard can be
  sent to server before paste, enabling proper bracketed paste support
- Update readClipboardCallback to return stored standard clipboard
- Use Inter/Source Code Pro/Roboto/Verdana font stack for shortcut display

feat(freeze): make _tsc work end-to-end and add dispatch caching (2.16x speedup)

Two major changes:

1. Fix native Math codegen bug: shouldSkipForNativeMath would skip
   get_var("Math") + get_field2("abs") but then force_stack_mode prevented
   tryEmitNativeMathCall from emitting the native replacement at call_method.
   Added native_math_skipped flag to bridge the gap.

2. Add frozen function dispatch caching: cache frozen function pointers
   directly in JSFunctionBytecode.frozen_impl to avoid hash map lookups
   on every call. NULL=not looked up, (void*)1=miss cached, other=fn ptr.
   Reduces _tsc runtime from 12.25s to 5.68s (2.16x faster).

fix(mux): fix consistent layout heights, restore titles on reload

- Fix titlebar/toolbar using fixed heights (28px/40px) so emoji titles
  don't cause layout shifts that change panel dimensions
- Use visibility:hidden instead of display:none for fullscreen hide so
  bars always occupy layout space
- Apply panel titles from PANEL_LIST message after layout restoration
  so tab titles persist across page reload
- Remove setTimeout from reconnect resize path for consistent timing

fix(mux): use surface dims for cursor percentage to fix max-pixel coordinate mismatch

When the encoder scales down the surface (MAX_PIXELS cap) or adds 16px
alignment padding, cursor coordinates in ghostty surface space don't
match decoded video frame dimensions. Use server surface dims instead
of frame dims for cursor percentage and CSS viewport aspect ratio.

fix(mux): move cursor rendering to CSS overlay, disable server-side cursor sprites

Ghostty cursor sprite functions (block, hollow, bar, underline) are now
no-ops — all cursor visuals are rendered via CSS overlay. Server always
sends cell-sized rectangle; CSS handles bar (max-width:2px), underline
(border-bottom), and inactive hollow rect (border, no blink). Cursor Y
offset +2 and height -2 for baseline alignment, width -1 for tighter fit.

fix(mux): fix cursor position/width with actual ghostty padding and CSS container queries

- Use ghostty's actual padding (padding_top_px, padding_left_px) instead of
  guessing centered padding, fixing the 0.4% vertical offset
- Fix bar cursor width from 2px to 1px to match ghostty's visual rendering
- Replace JS objFitScale cursor math with CSS container query viewport that
  replicates object-fit:contain sizing identically
- Rename inner .panel to .panel-root to fix cursor blink CSS selector
- Remove dead ghostty_stub import and debug console.log statements
- Extend ghostty SurfaceSize C API with padding fields (vendor patch)

fix(mux): fix H264 hardware decode stall with Constrained Baseline SPS

VAAPI hardware decoders buffer frames indefinitely in a reorder buffer
when the SPS advertises Main Profile without bitstream_restriction flags,
even with optimizeForLatency:true. Fix by advertising Constrained Baseline
(profile_idc=66) in the SPS with max_num_reorder_frames=0, while keeping
Main Profile for actual VAAPI encoding (Constrained Baseline encoding is
broken on many GPUs). Also add raw frame buffering on the client to cache
H264 frames before gpuRenderer is ready, and reset frame_count on IDR.

fix(mux): switch H264 decoder to avc3 + raw Annex B passthrough

Use avc3 codec with in-band SPS/PPS (Annex B format) instead of avc1
with out-of-band description. This eliminates the need for Annex B to
AVCC format conversion — server sends Annex B natively, client passes
it straight through to VideoDecoder. Simpler, less overhead.

fix(mux): consolidate to 2-WS architecture with zstd framing and no-drop message caching

Merge 4 WebSocket channels into 2 (H264 + Control/zstd). All control
messages are zstd-framed with [flag:u8][data...] in both directions.
Client outgoing messages go through a single rAF bus (controlOutQueue)
gated on zstd readiness. Incoming panel-specific messages (title, pwd,
bell, cursor, H264 frames) are cached when the panel isn't registered
yet and flushed on PANEL_CREATED.

Fix use-after-free crash on shutdown: add write_mutex to Connection that
serializes broadcast sendBinary calls with deinit, preventing access to
freed zstd compressor when connections tear down concurrently.

fix(mux): fix cursor positioning with surface-space coords and reconnect support

Send raw surface-space cursor coordinates instead of encoder-scaled values,
include server surface dimensions in the 19-byte cursor protocol so the
client can properly map surface → encoder → CSS display coordinates.
Send cursor state to newly connected control clients for reload support.

feat(mux): add frontend cursor blink, channel-based IPC, and stability fixes

- Add Channel(T) and LockFreeQueue(T) async primitives (from metal0)
- Replace 4 mutex-protected ArrayList MPSC queues with Channel(T) for
  pending panels/destroys/resizes/splits — each channel has its own
  mutex (less contention) and eliminates per-frame toOwnedSlice alloc
- Add cursor_state (0x14) control message: server sends cursor position
  after each frame, client renders CSS overlay with blink animation
- Fix segfault in onPanelDisconnect: validate panel pointer is still
  alive before accessing (prevents use-after-free during shutdown)
- Fix blank frame on new tab: reconfigure WebGPU context when canvas
  dimensions change before rendering
- Fix idle mode not updating after input: reset consecutive_unchanged
  counter in processInputQueue
- Remove loading overlay immediately when WebGPU renderer initializes
- Remove shutdown debug prints

feat(freeze): add inline caches for property access in relooper codegen

Add per-call-site inline caches (IC) for all get_field/get_field2
property accesses in the relooper codegen. Each IC slot caches
(shape_ptr, prop_offset, atom) - on cache hit, reads the property
directly from the object's prop array in Zig (zero FFI calls).
On miss, falls back to atom-cached JS_GetProperty.

- Add js_frozen_ic_load C function in QuickJS patch
- Add ICSlot struct and icLoad with Zig-inlined fast path
- Replace native cache special cases with uniform IC in relooper
- Pre-scan bytecode for IC slot count to size static arrays

Covers 5,814 property access sites across 350 relooper shards.
Performance is at parity (~13.5s) - property access caching is not
the bottleneck; C FFI overhead dominates the 13x gap vs Node.

perf(freeze): optimize _tsc shard compilation and codegen (~1.2x speedup)

Four optimizations to reduce frozen shard overhead:

1. Use frozen_optimize (ReleaseFast) for shard compilation instead of
   hardcoded ReleaseSmall, enabling LLVM to inline CV helper functions
   across the 900K+ fromJSValue/toJSValue calls in generated code.

2. Add primitive fast path for get_loc_check - skip the full
   CV→JSValue→dup→JSValue→CV round-trip for non-reference types
   (int, float, bool, null, undefined) which are the majority in TSC.

3. Right-size stack arrays using actual stack_size from bytecode metadata
   instead of hardcoded 256-element minimum. Most functions need <32 slots.

4. Extract exception cleanup into noinline runtime helper
   (zig_runtime.cleanupLocals) instead of inlining collect-then-free
   loop at every exception site, reducing code size for better icache.

Measured: ~16s → ~13.3s on _tsc test (1.20x improvement).

fix(freeze): add closure reverse sync and native shape cache coherence

Two correctness fixes for the frozen interpreter:

1. Closure variable reverse sync in relooper codegen: After function calls,
   closures may have written to captured parent locals through var_refs.
   The relooper was missing the reverse sync (_locals_jsv → locals) that
   the full codegen already had, causing stale local values and wrong
   type-checking paths (e.g., TS2345 crash). Added targeted sync that
   only copies the specific captured local indices, guarded by a runtime
   _closure_alive flag to avoid syncing before any closure exists.

2. Native shape cache write-through for flags: nativeGetFlags() reads from
   a cached NativeAstNode.flags, but put_field "flags" wrote only to the
   JS object via setPropertyStr, leaving the cache stale. This broke
   declare namespace auto-export: setExportContextFlag set ExportContext
   (flag 128) on the JS object, but declareModuleMember read the stale
   cached value without it, routing all namespace members to locals instead
   of exports. Added nativeSetFlags() that updates both the native cache
   and JS object, and wired put_field "flags" codegen to use it.

Fixes: declare namespace member resolution, Intl type errors in lib.es5.d.ts

feat(mux): wire io_uring/dispatch_io for maximum I/O throughput

Platform-optimal I/O paths with comptime selection:

Linux (io_uring):
- Large files: pipelined multi-chunk reads via IoUringReader
- Small files: batch openat/read/close via MultiFileReader
- File hashing: threaded ParallelHasher (up to 8 threads, strided)

macOS (dispatch_io/dispatch_apply):
- Large files: pipelined dispatch_io reads with semaphore sync
- Small files: parallel pread via dispatch_apply in MultiFileReader
- File hashing: dispatch_apply in ParallelHasher

Other platforms: mmap with madvise prefetch (fallback).

Also:
- Replace DispatchIO busy-wait (sleep 100ns) with dispatch_semaphore
- Add readFileViaUring (Linux) and readFileViaDispatchIO (macOS)
- Add buildFileListAsync: walk-then-batch-hash for faster file listing
- Add @compileError guards to prevent cross-platform misuse
- Fix IoUringReader.read buffer type for Zig 0.15.2 API

feat(mux): add transfer resume support and OPFS cache management UI

Transfer resume: server preserves transfer session state on file WS
disconnect, client tracks interrupted uploads and auto-resumes on
reconnect via extended TRANSFER_READY (25-byte format with resume
position). Server handles TRANSFER_RESUME (0x23) to restore sessions.

OPFS cache management: add Storage menu item under File menu to show
cache usage (file count, total size) and clear all cached files.
Worker implements recursive directory walk for usage calculation and
full cache directory removal for cleanup.

feat(mux): add OPFS cache and rsync delta sync protocol

OPFS cache layer persists downloaded files in the browser's Origin
Private File System for fast re-access. Rsync protocol enables
block-level delta sync — client sends rolling+strong checksums of
cached copies, server computes minimal COPY/LITERAL deltas, reducing
transfer size for incremental updates.

- OPFS cache: Worker-side sync access with main-thread promise wrapper
- Rsync messages: SYNC_REQUEST (0x25), BLOCK_CHECKSUMS (0x26),
  SYNC_ACK (0x27), SYNC_FILE_LIST (0x38), DELTA_DATA (0x39),
  SYNC_COMPLETE (0x3A)
- Server: rolling checksum hash table + delta computation, adaptive
  block size (sqrt clamped to 512-64KB)
- Client: startSync with cache comparison, checksum computation via
  Worker, delta application, automatic fallback to full download

feat(mux): add file transfer with Worker zstd, server push, and small file batching

Implement Phase 3 of file transfer protocol:
- Server pushes download data via pushDownloadFiles() instead of waiting
- Fix critical transfer ID mismatch (client Date.now() vs server sequential)
- Move zstd WASM to dedicated Web Worker with sync OPFS access
- Add BATCH_DATA (0x37) message for small file batching (<16KB)
- Server batches consecutive small files into single compressed blocks

feat(mux): add paste/drop file upload triggers and no-cache headers

Paste (Cmd+V) and drag-drop files onto any panel to trigger upload
dialog with the panel's CWD as default path. Text paste continues
to work as before. Add Cache-Control: no-store to prevent browsers
from serving stale terminal client after server stops.

fix(mux): fix panel scale/zoom on creation and after heavy output

Two fixes for wrong scale/aspect ratio:

1. Defer sendCreatePanel/sendSplitPanel via rAF when getBoundingClientRect
   returns 0 (tab content has display:none before selectTab adds .active).
   Previously fell back to 800x600 defaults, creating the ghostty surface
   at wrong dimensions.

2. Send buffer stats periodically via setInterval, not just on frame
   arrival. After heavy output (e.g. lsd), AIMD quality tier drops but
   couldn't recover during idle because checkBufferHealth was only called
   per-frame. Idle terminals now report good health so the server upgrades
   the tier back.

feat(mux): add dedicated /ws/file WebSocket for file transfers

Move file transfers off the control WebSocket to eliminate head-of-line
blocking. Large 256KB chunk transfers no longer starve control messages.

- Add /ws/file route to HTTP server path-based WebSocket routing
- Add file WS server with connect/message/disconnect callbacks
- Add lazy ensureFileWs() on client — connects only on first transfer
- Keep backwards-compatible fallback for 0x20-0x24 on control WS

feat(mux): add touch accessory bar and rewrite README

Accessory bar for mobile/touch devices with sticky modifiers (⇧⌃⌥⌘),
terminal keys (esc, tab, |, ~, -, /), and arrow keys. Bar uses
position:fixed with visualViewport tracking to stay above the iOS
keyboard and safe area. All elements are non-focusable (tabindex=-1)
to prevent stealing focus from the hidden textarea.

README rewritten to position termweb as a streaming multiplexer
("Stream Ghostty to any browser") with updated architecture diagram,
feature categories, and browser support info.

feat(mux): add mobile touch input and virtual keyboard support for iOS

Add hidden textarea for iOS virtual keyboard capture with sentinel-space
pattern for backspace detection. Touch events on canvas handle tap (mouse
click + keyboard focus), single-finger drag (mouse move), and two-finger
scroll. Characters are sent as KEY_INPUT (not TEXT_INPUT) so ghostty
processes them through its normal input handler, preserving cursor style
changes. Textarea tracks cursor position to prevent iOS viewport scroll.
Renderer selection is now logged to console for debugging.

feat(mux): add iOS Safari support with avc1 codec and Canvas 2D fallback

Safari rejects avc3 codec strings in VideoDecoder — switch to avc1 with
AVCDecoderConfigurationRecord description and in-place Annex B → AVCC
frame conversion. Add Canvas 2D renderer fallback for browsers without
WebGPU (iOS Safari). Remove navigator.gpu gate from Panel init.

chore(mux): remove verbose debug prints from server

Remove FORCE_KF, ENCODER, QUALITY, ADAPTIVE, H264 client
connect/disconnect, and Ghostty init/free debug messages that
were cluttering server output during normal operation.
fix(mux): make menu text non-selectable

fix(mux): implement split navigation and resize commands

Previous/next split commands were falling through to zoomSplit().
Wire them to selectAdjacentSplit() which cycles through panels in
DFS order. Also implement directional selection, equalize, and
resize split commands that were previously stubbed as TODO.

- Handle INSPECTOR_OPEN_STATE (0x1E) to restore inspector on page reload
- Send SET_INSPECTOR (0x8B) to persist open/closed state on server

fix(mux): fix font size actions and keyboard shortcuts with Alt modifier

- Use e.code instead of e.key for Alt-modified shortcuts (quick terminal,

fix(mux): fix close-panel double-fire, tab overview rendering, and live preview streaming

- Guard closePanel against double-fire from Svelte event delegation with closeInProgress flag
- Select adjacent sibling panel (not first-in-tree) as active after closing a panel
- Show snapshot as base layer in tab overview with H264 canvas overlay on top
- Force keyframe on all panels when overview opens for immediate decoder start
