Escape should be passed through to the terminal, not intercepted to
close the quick terminal panel.

fix(mux): re-send cursor state on surface resize to fix stale coordinates

When a panel's surface resizes (e.g., after a split), SURFACE_DIMS was
sent with updated dimensions but CURSOR_STATE was only re-sent when
the cursor's grid col/row changed. This left the client with stale
pixel coordinates computed against the old surface layout, causing the
cursor to appear at wrong scale and position until the user typed.

feat(mux): add AIMD tiered adaptive quality with shared pixel budget

Replace the old per-panel static MAX_PIXELS cap and linear quality
adjustment with a proper AIMD (Additive Increase, Multiplicative
Decrease) tiered system that shares a total pixel budget across all
actively rendering panels proportional to their visible area.

5 quality tiers (bitrate + resolution cap + FPS):
- Tier 0 Emergency: 1Mbps, 1024x768, 15fps
- Tier 1 Low: 2Mbps, 1280x1024, 24fps
- Tier 2 Medium: 3Mbps, 1920x1080, 30fps
- Tier 3 High (default): 5Mbps, 2048x2048, 30fps
- Tier 4 Max: 8Mbps, 3840x2160, 30fps

AIMD stability: fast drop (2 tiers after 2s bad health), slow recovery
(1 tier after 3s good + 5s stability timer), dead zone prevents
oscillation. Pixel budget distributed proportionally by panel visible
area — single panel gets full budget, splits share proportionally,
no downscale when total fits within budget.

fix: resolve frozen dispatch naming mismatch for anonymous functions

The ChildFuncInfo.name for anonymous functions was set to 'anonymous'
(via getAtomString fallback) while dispatch registration used '' (empty
string via getAtomByIndex). This caused 156K 'Name not found' errors
in registerCpoolBytecodeByName, preventing bytecode pointer registration
for anonymous closures.

Changes:
- module_parser.zig: Use getAtomByIndex instead of getAtomString for
  ChildFuncInfo naming, matching the AnalyzedFunction convention
- native_dispatch.zig: Strip 'anonymous' prefix in cpool lookup for
  backward compatibility with existing pre-built shards

Results: fclosure misses reduced 156K -> 46K (71% reduction),
bytecode registrations up from 2559 -> 2765.

fix: shared profile counters across frozen shards via C extern linkage

Root cause: each frozen shard was compiled as a separate object file, giving
each its own copy of the profile counter variables. Increments in shard code
wrote to shard-local copies that were never read by the print function.

Fix:
- Add profile_counters.c with single definition of counter array and vinc()
- Update profile.zig to declare counters/vinc as extern C symbols
- Update all callers (zig_runtime.zig, js_value.zig) to use new accessor API
- Add profile_counters.c to all build targets (native, native-static, wasm-static, wasm-standalone)

Results now show real profiling data:
- 17.7M function calls from frozen functions
- 34% IC hit rate (436K/1.26M property accesses)
- 123M CV conversions, 54M dups / 63M frees

fix(mux): restart cursor blink animation on position change

Use Svelte {#key} block keyed on cursor coordinates to recreate the
cursor DOM element on each move, restarting the CSS blink animation
so the cursor is always visible immediately after repositioning.

fix(mux): poll cursor state before frame hash-skip to fix space tracking

Cursor state polling was placed after the hash-skip `continue`, so
invisible characters (spaces) that don't change the framebuffer would
never trigger a cursor position update to the frontend CSS overlay.
debug(mux): add shutdown tracing to identify double Ctrl+C hang point

fix(mux): fix goroutine starvation after cancel and metadata race

Two fixes:

1. Cancelled goroutines now check is_active BEFORE acquiring the
   semaphore. Previously 2,499 cancelled goroutines had to drain
   through 32 semaphore slots before new transfer goroutines could
   start, blocking downloads for seconds.

2. Worker tracks cancelledTransfers Set to prevent async chunk-write
   completions from re-creating OPFS metadata after delete. The race
   was: cancel deletes metadata, then in-flight decompress-and-write
   promise resolves and calls updateTransferProgress which re-saves.

fix(mux): prevent duplicate file completion counting per chunk

When a file already exists at full size in OPFS (from a previous
download or resume), every chunk write triggered complete=true since
handle.getSize() >= fileSize. This caused the same file to be counted
multiple times in filesCompleted, stalling progress at wrong %.

Fix: track completed files in both worker (completedFiles Set) and
main thread (transfer.completedPaths Set). Skip processing once a
file has been reported complete.

fix(mux): fix segfault and double Ctrl+C on shutdown

Push threads were detached, causing use-after-free when deinit freed
sessions while threads still accessed session.is_active. Now track
thread handles and join them during shutdown.

Also signal goroutine runtime shutdown in run() so GChannel.recv()
unblocks push threads immediately (fixes double Ctrl+C).

perf(mux): skip frame encoding when only cursor blinks

Hash the framebuffer excluding the cursor cell region so cursor blink
doesn't trigger unnecessary frame encodes. The frontend renders the
cursor via CSS overlay, so blink changes in the pixel buffer are waste.

fix(mux): increase worker decompression buffer and fix byte counting

BATCH_DATA decompression was silently failing because the worker's
MAX_DECOMPRESSED_SIZE (16MB) was too small for large batches (30MB+).
Increase to 128MB. Also fix double-counting of bytesTransferred by
only counting small files in handleCompletedFile since large files
are already counted in onChunkWrittenToOPFS.

fix(mux): fix transfer cancel, resume ID mismatch, and shutdown hangs

Fixes critical transfer bugs:
- Cancel button now sends TRANSFER_CANCEL to server and stops goroutines
- Server uses client's transfer ID for resume (fixes ID mismatch bug)
- Skip duplicate resume attempts when transfer already active
- Cancel all transfers before shutdown (fixes double Ctrl+C requirement)
- Stop goroutines immediately on client disconnect
- Delete OPFS metadata on cancel to prevent infinite resume loops

Before: clicking cancel only updated client state, server kept running.
After: server receives TRANSFER_CANCEL and sets is_active = false to stop
all goroutines and threads immediately.

Before: resume created new server ID (5) while client expected original ID (3),
causing "No transfer found for ID" errors and empty transfer monitor.
After: server reuses client's transfer ID via createSessionWithId().

Before: required double Ctrl+C because http_thread.join() blocked waiting
for transfer threads to finish, which waited for slow file I/O goroutines.
After: cancelAll() called before join() to exit goroutines quickly.

fix(mux): thread file list building for downloads and uploads

Fixed critical blocking operations that froze WebSocket receive loop for 3-7
seconds on large directories (14,000+ files):

**Threaded Operations:**
1. handleTransferInit() - Initial download file list
   - Was blocking 3-7s during buildFileListAsync()
   - Now spawns buildFileListAndSendThread()

2. handleFileListRequest() - Upload file list
   - Was blocking during directory scan for uploads
   - Now spawns buildFileListForUploadThread()

**Impact:**
Before: WS receive loop frozen during file list building
- ❌ Can't receive new messages
- ❌ Can't process shutdown signals
- ❌ Can't handle concurrent transfers

After: Non-blocking file list building
- ✅ WS loop stays responsive
- ✅ Shutdown works during file scanning
- ✅ Concurrent transfers supported

Changes:
- main.zig:3785: Add buildFileListAndSendThread() for downloads
- main.zig:3330: Thread initial download file list building
- main.zig:3364: Add buildFileListForUploadThread() for uploads
- main.zig:3362: Thread upload file list building

Note: Resume and sync handlers still block (less critical, will fix separately).

fix(mux): remove document.title updates during downloads

Removed all document.title updates that were overriding other page titles.
The title updates for download progress were:
- Showing file progress during active transfers
- Resetting to 'termweb' on completion/cancel/error

Users found this intrusive when multiple tabs/features set custom titles.

Changes:
- App.svelte:508-517: Removed progress title update
- App.svelte:539-543: Removed completion title reset
- App.svelte:551-555: Removed cancel title reset
- App.svelte:555-559: Removed error title reset

Result: Page title is no longer modified by download progress.

fix(mux): unblock shutdown and fix resume threading

Fixed two issues causing slow/double Ctrl+C shutdown:

1. Channel not closed during shutdown:
   - Defer block now closes channel when session.is_active becomes false
   - This unblocks the recv() loop which was waiting forever
   - Single Ctrl+C now works cleanly

2. Resume handler blocking WebSocket thread:
   - handleTransferResume() was calling pushDownloadFiles() synchronously
   - This blocked the WS receive loop, preventing it from processing shutdown
   - Now spawns thread like initial download handler does
   - Moved debug print before thread spawn to avoid accessing freed session

Changes:
- main.zig:3835: Close channel in defer when exiting early
- main.zig:3537-3545: Thread pushDownloadFiles for resume

Result: Single Ctrl+C cleanly shuts down even during active transfers.

feat(mux): apply 50MB file size limit to uploads and file lists

Extended the 50MB file size limit to all transfer operations:

Before: Large files were only rejected during download processing, but still
included in file lists and accepted for upload. This meant:
- Upload file list could include 100MB+ files
- Client would attempt to upload them (wasting bandwidth)
- Server would accept and write them (disk exhaustion risk)

Now: Large files are filtered out early during file list building:
- walkDirectory(): Skip files > 50MB during directory scan
- walkDirectoryNoHash(): Skip files > 50MB in async version
- buildSingleFileEntry(): Reject single file if > 50MB

Changes:
- transfer.zig:1417: Add size check in walkDirectory (sync)
- transfer.zig:1599: Add size check in walkDirectoryNoHash (async)
- transfer.zig:1512: Add size check in buildSingleFileEntry

Result:
- Uploads are protected from large files (prevents disk exhaustion)
- File lists exclude large files (saves bandwidth)
- Clear log messages show what's skipped and why

fix(mux): graceful shutdown during active transfers

Fixed double Ctrl+C requirement when transfers are in progress. The detached
pushDownloadFiles thread was blocking shutdown by waiting for all goroutines
to complete, even when the server was shutting down.

The thread now checks session.is_active during the goroutine wait loop and
exits early when cancelAll() is called during deinit().

Change:
- main.zig:3831: Add session.is_active check in defer goroutine wait loop

Result: Single Ctrl+C now cleanly shuts down the server.

feat(mux): add 50MB file size limit to prevent OOM crashes

Very large files (>50MB) are now skipped during download to prevent memory
exhaustion. Previously, files of any size were loaded entirely into memory:
- 100 MB file = 100 MB allocated
- 32 concurrent goroutines on 100 MB files = 3.2 GB memory
- Plus compression buffers = OOM crash

The 248 MB .zig-cache files were causing the server to be killed.

Changes:
- transfer.zig: Add max_file_size constant (50 MB)
- main.zig: Check file size in readFileForGoroutine()
- Skipped files logged with clear message showing size and limit

Future enhancement: Implement streaming for very large files instead of skipping.

feat(mux): add auto-resume on page load and prevent OOM with semaphore

Fixed two critical issues with large file downloads:

1. Auto-resume on page load:
   - App.svelte now calls ensureFileWs() after initialization
   - Opens file WebSocket automatically to trigger resume logic
   - Previously resume only triggered when manually starting a transfer

2. Memory exhaustion prevention:
   - Added semaphore with 32 permits to limit concurrent goroutines
   - Prevents spawning 500+ goroutines simultaneously for large directories
   - Each goroutine acquires semaphore before processing, releases after done
   - Server was being OOM-killed when downloading .zig-cache (500+ large files)

Changes:
- App.svelte: Call ensureFileWs() in onMount after callback setup
- main.zig: Add goroutine_sem semaphore to FileGoroutineCtx
- main.zig: processFileGoroutine() waits/posts on semaphore

Result:
- Downloads auto-resume after page reload
- Server stays stable even with massive directories

fix(mux): prevent server crash from invalid paths in goroutines

Fixed critical race condition and crash when downloading large directories:

1. Race condition: base_path was borrowed from session, causing dangling
   pointer when goroutines ran on different threads. Now each goroutine
   owns a copy of base_path.

2. Server crash: openDirAbsolute() would panic if base_path wasn't absolute.
   Added validation that logs error and continues instead of crashing.

Changes:
- FileGoroutineCtx: base_path is now owned (duplicated)
- deinit(): frees base_path
- pushDownloadFiles(): duplicates base_path when creating goroutine context
- readFileForGoroutine(): validates base_path is absolute before use

This ensures server stability - fails gracefully with error logs instead
of crashing on invalid data.

fix(mux): prevent WebSocket receive loop blocking during file transfers

**Problem:**
Consecutive file downloads would hang because pushDownloadFiles() blocked
the WebSocket receive thread for 5+ seconds, preventing new TRANSFER_INIT
messages from being processed.

**Root Cause:**
handleTransferInit() called pushDownloadFiles() synchronously on the
WebSocket receive thread. While waiting for goroutines to finish file
compression/sending, the receive loop couldn't read new messages from
the TCP buffer, causing subsequent downloads to timeout.

**Solution:**
Spawn pushDownloadFiles() in a separate OS thread (line 3358 of main.zig)
so the WebSocket receive loop can continue processing incoming messages
while file transfers run in the background.

**Architecture:**
- OS thread: Runs pushDownloadFiles() to unblock WebSocket receive loop
- Greenthreads/Goroutines: Used by pushDownloadFiles() for parallel I/O
  (compressing 49+ files concurrently)

This hybrid approach leverages both thread types for their strengths:
OS threads for system-level blocking operations, goroutines for concurrent
application-level I/O.

**Changes:**
- main.zig: Thread pushDownloadFiles() to avoid blocking receive loop
- file-transfer.ts: OPFS persistence for download resume across page reloads
- transfer.zig: Server-side TRANSFER_RESUME protocol support
- mux.ts: Remove onConnectionShouldClose callback (rely on idle timer)

fix(cursor): use frame dimensions for cursor viewport to fix 16px alignment drift

On Linux, VA-API requires 16-pixel aligned frames, so the video frame
can be slightly larger than the ghostty surface (e.g. 2560x1264 vs
2560x1254). The cursor viewport CSS was using surface dims for its
object-fit:contain emulation, creating an aspect ratio mismatch with
the canvas content and causing cursor drift (~0.6% at the bottom).

Use video frame dimensions for the viewport aspect ratio so it matches
the canvas exactly. For cursor percentages, detect the encoder path:
fast path (surfW == frameW, direct copy + padding) uses frame dims;
downscale path (surfW != frameW, stretched to fill) uses surface dims.
Falls back to surface dims before the first video frame arrives.

fix(cursor): update cursor position on zoom in/out/reset

Cell dimensions (cell_width_px, cell_height_px) change on font size
zoom but the cursor re-send condition only checked surface pixel
dimensions and cursor cell position. When the surface stays the same
size and the cursor cell doesn't move, the cursor was never re-sent
despite its pixel position being stale. Track last_cell_w/h and
include cell dimension changes in the re-send condition.

test(mux): add integration test for tmux shim Unix socket API

Shell script that starts termweb mux, exercises all tmux shim commands
(has-session, list-panes, display-message, split-window, new-window,
send-keys, select-pane, -V) via both the shim script and raw curl
over Unix socket. Verifies socket permissions, response formats, and
script content. Run with: make test-tmux

feat(auth): add per-IP rate limiting for failed auth attempts

Block IPs after 10 failed auth attempts within a 5-minute window.
Lockout expires automatically after 5 minutes, and successful auth
resets the counter. Covers both HTTP and WebSocket auth paths.

feat(auth): add token-based authentication for all connections

Require valid tokens for HTTP, WebSocket (control, H264, file transfer)
connections. Server generates admin share link and editor session token
at startup, printing percent-encoded URLs.

Key changes:
- auth.zig: remove auth_required bypass, add decodeToken (caller-provided
  buffer for thread safety), percentEncodeToken for URL-safe output,

  client.js into index.html to avoid unauthenticated sub-resource fetches

  fetches (WASM, workers, config), editors stay in main mode (only
  read-only viewers use viewer mode)

fix(cursor): align CSS cursor color and alpha with Ghostty native rendering

Extend the cursor state protocol to include RGB color from Ghostty's
resolved cursor color chain (OSC 12 → cursor-color config → foreground).
Fix character disappearing under block cursor when cursor-opacity=0 by
skipping both cursor glyph and text recoloring when fully transparent.

- Handle optional process in stop(), readerThread, and main.zig

fix(mux): fix transfer resume, session cleanup, and paste upload

- Fix segfault: session use-after-free in pushDownloadFiles and
  buildFileListAndSendThread by centralizing cleanup in defers
  with mutex-protected conn.user_data access
- Fix segfault: sendDryRunReport double-free by removing its
  removeSession call (defer handles it)
- Fix download resume ignoring useGitignore/excludes: persist
  transfer options in OPFS metadata, include flags+excludes in
  TRANSFER_RESUME protocol, parse and apply on server
- Fix OPFS cleanup: cancelTransfer now always deletes metadata
  even after onDisconnect clears activeTransfers; cleanup-temp
  handler now removes OPFS temp files (was only clearing memory)
- Fix memory leak on shutdown: track buildFileListAndSendThread
  for join (was detached), add is_active early-abort in

  during resume filter
- Fix folder paste upload: use getAsFileSystemHandle() in paste
  handler to detect directories (matching drop handler pattern)
- Fix missing serverPath in upload pendingTransfer
- Add build_options module for mux benchmark gating

fix(mux): filter gitignored files during upload and gate benchmark counters

- Make loadGitignore public so upload handler can call it

- Gate all bandwidth benchmark counters behind -Dbenchmark build flag

fix(mux): detect dropped folders via getAsFileSystemHandle

Dragging a folder onto the terminal created a File object that couldn't
be read via arrayBuffer(), causing upload failure. Now uses the File
System Access API to detect directory handles and routes them through
startFolderUpload instead of startFilesUpload.

fix(mux): fix file upload by adding UPLOAD_FILE_LIST protocol message

Uploads were broken because the server's handleFileData checked
file_index against an empty session.files list (only populated for
downloads). Added UPLOAD_FILE_LIST (0x25) message so the client sends
its file list to the server before uploading data. Also added upload
progress tracking via onTransferStart and onDownloadProgress callbacks.

fix(mux): handle server-initiated panel creation in web client

When the tmux shim API creates panels (split-window, new-window), the
server broadcasts PANEL_CREATED + LAYOUT_UPDATE. The client previously
only reconciled direction/ratio for existing panels, silently ignoring
new panels. Now detects unknown panel IDs in the layout and does a full
rebuild via restoreLayoutFromServer.

refactor(transfer): remove deleteExtra option from file transfer

Simplify transfer flags by removing the unused delete-extra-files feature.
Shifts flag bits so dry_run=bit0 and useGitignore=bit1.

feat(mux): add tmux shim with Unix domain socket for Claude Code agent teams

Intercepts tmux commands from Claude Code's teams feature and routes them
to termweb's mux system via a private Unix socket at /tmp/termweb-{pid}/tmux.sock.
File permissions (owner-only) provide security without needing auth tokens.

fix(chrome): fix download source_path memory leak and clean up temp dir on exit

Always free the source_path string after use (the file on disk is
separate from the string). Remove toolbar "Saved: ..." notification
on download complete. Delete the per-process temp download directory
on viewer shutdown.

fix(chrome): use unique temp dir for downloads and platform-specific save handling

Downloads now use a per-process temp directory ({TMPDIR}/termweb-dl-{pid})
instead of a fixed /tmp/termweb-downloads path. On Linux, completed
downloads stay in the temp dir with the path copied to clipboard via
OSC 52 (zero external dependencies). On macOS, the existing osascript
save dialog is preserved unchanged.

fix(mux): fix single-file download race between BATCH_DATA and TRANSFER_COMPLETE

handleBatchData is async (awaits decompress), which yields control to the
event loop. During that await, handleTransferComplete can delete the transfer
from activeTransfers (non-zip single-file path). When handleBatchData resumes,
the transfer is gone and handleCompletedFile fails with "No transfer found".

Fix by saving the files reference before the async gap and re-checking the
transfer afterward. If it was deleted, fall back to saveFile() directly.

fix(mux): move OPFS init to main thread and use worker memory for downloads

navigator.storage.getDirectory() hangs in workers in some environments.
Move the call to the main thread where it resolves reliably, then send
the root handle to the worker via postMessage (structured clone).

Replace OPFS temp file storage with in-memory Map in the worker for the
download→decompress→zip flow. Remove all main-thread fallback paths
(compressionPool, accumulateAndSave, chunk-decompressed) so there is
one code path: worker WASM decompression + worker memory storage.

feat(mux): add idle quality keyframe after activity bursts

When AIMD degrades quality during heavy terminal output, the user was
stuck with a blurry/blocky screen until the next periodic keyframe
(every 600 frames = ~20s). Now, after 15 consecutive unchanged frames
(~0.5s at 30 FPS), a one-shot quality keyframe is forced so the screen
sharpens quickly after activity stops. The flag resets when new content
arrives, allowing the next idle period to trigger another keyframe.

feat(mux): send keyboard and mouse-click input immediately, bypassing rAF batching

All interactive input (keystrokes, mouse clicks, touch taps, text input)
now sends directly on the control WebSocket instead of being queued for
the next requestAnimationFrame flush. This eliminates 0-16ms (~8ms avg)
of latency per interactive event. Small messages (<32 bytes) skip zstd
compression entirely (flag 0x00), avoiding overhead on tiny keystrokes.

Bulk/non-interactive messages (mouse move, scroll, resize, layout,
clipboard) remain rAF-batched for efficiency.

refactor(auth): one token per session, role server-side, per-session JWT signing

Replace two-token-per-session model (editor + viewer) with single 256-bit
permanent token. Role is now a server-side session attribute instead of being
encoded in tokens. JWTs contain only session_id + expiry, signed with the
session's permanent token as HMAC-SHA256 key (no separate jwt_secret).

Server (auth.zig, main.zig, http_server.zig, cli.zig):
- Remove TokenType enum, token prefixes, and jwt_secret
- Session struct: editor_token/viewer_token → token (32 bytes) + role
- ShareLink: token_type → role
- JWT two-pass validation: parse session_id, look up session, verify signature
- Wire format: 64-char hex token + role byte
- Backwards-compatible auth.json migration (old format discarded)

Client (types.ts, mux.ts, App.svelte, AdminSharesDialog.svelte):
- Session: editorToken/viewerToken → token + role
- Protocol parser updated for new wire format
- ShareDialog shows spinner while fetching share URL from server

feat(auth): add login page, GitHub/Google OAuth, and PWA support

Serve a login page instead of 401 when visiting without a token.
Support authentication via permanent token, GitHub OAuth, and Google
OAuth — all methods create a JWT and redirect to /?token=<jwt>.
Add admin UI for configuring OAuth provider credentials. Serve PWA
manifest without auth for installability.

fix(mux): clear ghostty default keybindings for web context

Add keybind=clear to termweb defaults so ghostty's desktop bindings
(Alt+F4, Ctrl+Insert, etc.) don't leak into the web terminal. User's
ghostty config still overrides our web presets via config load order.

fix(mux): fix reversed scroll, Shift+key input, and transfer init crash

- Fix reversed scroll direction: negate browser deltaX/deltaY before
  sending to ghostty (browser positive=down, ghostty positive=up)
- Fix scroll speed: send precision flag from browser so ghostty treats
  pixel-mode deltas (trackpad) correctly instead of multiplying by cell
  height
- Fix Shift+letter not producing uppercase: set consumed_mods to include
  Shift when text is produced, so ghostty's effectiveMods() doesn't
  treat Shift as an active modifier for encoding
- Fix transfer init crash: use std.meta.intToEnum instead of
  @enumFromInt to gracefully handle invalid direction bytes

perf(mux): reduce keypress-to-display latency from 33ms to ~5ms

After a keystroke wakes the render loop, pull last_frame backward so
the next frame boundary arrives within 5ms instead of up to 33ms.
Uses the normal 30fps frame capture path — no extra encoder calls.
Only triggers for keypress events, not mouse or other input.

feat(mux): add config editor, tab reorder, screen dump, and fix new tab rendering

- Add web-based config editor dialog (open_config reads file from server,
  edits in browser textarea, saves back with reload)
- Implement move_tab with server-side Layout.moveTab() and layout broadcast
- Implement write_screen_file/write_selection_file as browser downloads
  (server intercepts ghostty temp file, sends content to client)
- Fix new tab not rendering by setting layout.active_panel_id on panel
  creation so encode loop includes the new tab immediately
- Fix tab visibility using CSS visibility:hidden instead of display:none
  to prevent WebGPU 0x0 canvas initialization
- Fix tab ID mismatch between client/server with remap logic in
  handleLayoutUpdate
- Fix double-free crash in parseUploadFileList (folder upload) by using
  errdefer with initialized counter instead of manual free
- Remove duplicate goto_split commands from command palette
- Remove CSP frame-ancestors header blocking VS Code Simple Browser
- Clean up HTTP server headers (remove COOP/COEP, rewrite response logic)
- Add server-driven keybinding config with embedded HTML delivery

Replace vendored SDK copy with 80-line patch file

Pin ai@6.0.86 and use postinstall script to generate ai-patched.mjs
from the stock SDK + ai-vectorjson.patch. Reduces committed code
from 13K lines to 80 lines and ensures the patch stays in sync
with the pinned SDK version.

Add Vercel AI SDK benchmark with proper VectorJSON streaming patch

Replace the fake simulation benchmarks with a real Vercel AI SDK
comparison. The patched SDK uses VectorJSON's incremental streaming
parser (parser.feed(chunk)) instead of the stock O(n²)
accumulatedText + parsePartialJson pattern, achieving 100-300x
speedups on 100-500KB payloads.

- bench/vercel-sdk: real SDK benchmark with MockLanguageModelV3
- ai-patched.mjs: surgical patch of streamObject transform
- test/memory.mjs: JS heap + WASM memory tracking tests
- src/zig/simd.zig: SIMD acceleration module
- Rename .close() → .free() in bench/partial-access.mjs
- Remove obsolete bench/stress-test.mjs

Consolidate dual Proxy paths into shared factory functions

Extract createArrayProxy() and createObjectProxy() factories that
encapsulate the common Proxy handler logic (get, has, ownKeys,
getOwnPropertyDescriptor) shared between wrapDoc and wrapGC.
Both wrappers now build lightweight config objects with backend-specific
callbacks and delegate to the factories, eliminating ~115 lines of
duplicated Proxy boilerplate.

Add batch iteration exports to eliminate O(N²) sequential access

- Add doc_array_elements/doc_object_keys: walk container once, return
  all child tape indices in a static 64KB batch buffer (16K entries)
- Update JS Proxy handlers to precompute indices via single WASM call
- Update deepMaterializeDoc to use batch reads
- Add generation-based tracking to prevent stale FinalizationRegistry
  callbacks from freeing reused document slots
- Add close()/Symbol.dispose for explicit document disposal in tight loops

Full-access benchmark: 7 ops/s → 55 ops/s (7.6x improvement).
Partial-access: 1.6-2x faster than JSON.parse across all patterns.

Add VectorJSON: SIMD-accelerated JSON parser with WasmGC bridge

Full implementation including:
- Zig engine with zimdjson SIMD parsing (wasm32 + simd128)
- WAT WasmGC bridge for zero-copy lazy proxies
- Path B tape-direct navigation with document slots
- Proxy caching (element/property) to avoid O(N²) re-lookups
- Explicit disposal (close/Symbol.dispose) with generation tracking
- FinalizationRegistry fallback for automatic cleanup
- Streaming parser (chunked feed with status API)
- Stringify, deep compare, and JSON Schema validation
- Comprehensive test suite (375 tests across 8 files)
- Benchmarks: partial-access shows 1.7-2x speedup over JSON.parse

Add .gitignore for Zig/Node.js/WASM project

Ignore build artifacts (dist/, zig-out/, .zig-cache/),
dependencies (node_modules/, deps/), and OS/editor files.

Skip classify_input on happy path: try doc_parse first, 2x faster parse

Restructure parse() to attempt doc_parse directly. Only fall back to
classify_input when doc_parse fails (incomplete, trailing content, or
invalid input). Eliminates redundant full-input scan for valid JSON.

Also wire doc_stringify into vj.stringify() for doc-backed proxies —
one WASM call walks the tape directly instead of traversing Proxy traps.
Add end-to-end benchmark: parse → JSON.stringify vs JSON.parse → JSON.stringify

Unified parse() API: return status-based ParseResult instead of throwing

Replace the throwing parse() with a status-based API that returns
{status, value, remaining?, error?} where status is one of
"complete", "complete_early", "incomplete", or "invalid". This enables
graceful handling of partial JSON (e.g. AI SDK token-by-token output)
without a separate streaming API.

Zig engine additions:
- classify_input: lightweight O(n) structural scanner (bracket depth,
  string state, escape tracking) that classifies input without parsing
- autocomplete_input: appends closing tokens (quotes, nulls, braces)
  to incomplete JSON in-place in the WASM buffer
- get_value_end: returns byte offset where the first complete value
  ends, for complete_early slicing

zimdjson string metadata refactor:
- Move per-string metadata (input offset, raw length, has_escapes) to
  a separate string_meta array indexed by ordinal, replacing the
  inline header approach for cleaner separation
- Store full string length in a single u32 header instead of split
  u16+u24 encoding

All existing tests updated: parse() calls now access .value; error
tests check status instead of catching SyntaxError. New 103-test suite
covers all four statuses, edge cases, proxy behavior, and stress tests.
Remove eager mode benchmark and all eager references

Per-string escape dispatch: JSON.parse for escaped, input.slice for clean

Strings without escape sequences use V8 SlicedString via input.slice()
(zero character copy). Strings with escapes use JSON.parse on the
original input slice including quotes, leveraging V8's native unescaping.

Key changes:
- String buffer header expanded from 6 to 10 bytes to store raw_input_len
  (byte count between quotes in original input) alongside packed_offset
- Fix input offset computation: use structural token index instead of
  pointer arithmetic, which broke for tokens resolved from the padding
  buffer (when assume_padding=false)
- Fix string_buffer.strings length tracking in non-streaming FullParser:
  advanceString moves strings_ptr but never updated list length, causing
  doc_read_column_str_raw to return 0 for non-ASCII inputs
- doc_read_column_str_slices now outputs per-string raw_len|escape_bit,
  enabling fine-grained dispatch without whole-column fallback

Zero-copy string slicing: store input offsets in parser, slice from original JS string

Modify zimdjson parser to record each string's byte offset within the
original JSON input. For ASCII inputs without escape sequences (the
common case), string column access now does originalInput.slice() instead
of WASM memcpy + TextDecoder — V8 creates SlicedString objects with zero
character copying.

Key changes:
- writeString returns WriteResult {dst_end, src_end} for escape detection
- visitString stores packed (input_offset | has_escapes_bit) in string_buffer
- New doc_read_column_str_slices export outputs input offsets per column
- JS keeps original input string, uses slice() for unescaped ASCII strings
- Falls back to TextDecoder path for escaped or non-ASCII strings

Results: for...of destructure 0.95x (beats JSON.parse), was 1.12x.

Unify string path on raw UTF-8, add fast iterator, cache column refs

- Add doc_read_column_str_raw (Zig): copies raw UTF-8 bytes via memcpy
  instead of SIMD UTF-8→UTF-16 conversion. Computes UTF-16 code unit
  offsets for correct JS String.slice. ~30% faster string columns.
- Add doc_read_string_raw (Zig): single-call raw UTF-8 string read,
  writes ptr+len to batch_buffer.
- Replace all JS string reads with TextDecoder('utf-8') on raw bytes.
  Remove utf16Decoder, utf16ToString, utf16BulkToString, readUtf16Result,
  docReadStringUtf16 and related dead type declarations.
- Fast custom iterator: for...of bypasses Proxy get trap entirely
  (1 Proxy call vs N), saving ~40ns per element.
- Cache column reference in getter closure, eliminating Map.get per access.

Results (1000 items × 5 props):
  for...of destructure: 1.15x (was 1.56x)
  for-index destructure: 1.20x (was 1.56x)
  for...of 3 nums: 0.78x (beats JSON.parse)

Revert to cursor approach, document structuredClone limitation

Constructor-based per-row objects (2.89x iterate, 3.42x stringify)
were slower than cursor+Proxy (1.56x iterate, 2.71x stringify).
Creating 1000 objects upfront costs more than Proxy trap overhead.

Documents structuredClone limitation: Proxy objects cannot be cloned,
use materialize() first. Recommends destructuring for safe value
capture from cursor.

Update parse() JSDoc: recommend destructuring for cursor pattern

Destructuring (`const { id, name } = data[i]`) captures plain values
that are independent of the cursor — safe to store and faster than
spread. Updated docs to recommend this as the primary pattern.

Remove toJSON hack, let JSON.stringify walk Proxy natively

Removing toJSON from array/object proxies and the cursor row view
lets JSON.stringify traverse the columnar getters directly instead
of materializing the full object tree first. This drops round-trip
(parse + JSON.stringify) from 8.3x to 2.8x vs native JSON.

Documents the cursor pattern in parse() JSDoc: sequential access
and JSON.stringify work correctly, but stored references share the
same cursor. Users should destructure or call materialize() when
they need independent copies.

Add wasm-opt -O3 build step and batch string slice bridge exports

Integrate Binaryen wasm-opt -O3 into build pipeline (ReleaseSmall →
wasm-opt), shrinking engine.wasm 25% (183KB → 137KB). Add WAT bridge
exports for WASM-side batch string slicing (batchSliceStrings,
externArrayGet, externArrayLen) with jsstr imports.

Batch SIMD string columns: one-big-string + slice() + TextDecoder

Your idea: ONE WASM call SIMD-converts ALL strings into contiguous
UTF-16 buffer, JS creates ONE big string via TextDecoder('utf-16le'),
then .slice() each substring (zero-copy sliced strings in V8/JSC).

TextDecoder is 1.8x faster than chunked fromCharCode for bulk UTF-16.

Real string usage (String().length): 5.01x → 1.59x vs JSON.parse
Non-string props: 0.92x (8% FASTER than JSON.parse)
Single number prop: 0.79x (21% FASTER than JSON.parse)

Add batch SIMD string column export, keep WasmString for deferred conversion

Added doc_read_column_str_utf16 (Zig) for SIMD batch string→UTF-16
conversion. Testing showed eager JS string creation (665µs) is slower
than deferred WasmString wrappers (281µs) because String.fromCharCode
has high per-string overhead.

Kept WasmString approach: 1 WASM call (batch ptr/len) + N createGCString.
Batch SIMD UTF-16 export retained for future use when eager strings needed.

Batch string column reads: 3N → 1+N WASM calls

Use doc_read_column_str to get all (ptr, len) pairs in ONE WASM call,
then createGCString per string. Reduces string column WASM crossings
from 3000 to 1001 for 1000 items.

Full 5-prop access: 1.36x → 1.23x vs JSON.parse
3 non-string props: 0.96x → 0.93x (7% faster than JSON.parse)

Add SoA columnar reads with batch WASM exports

Columnar (Struct-of-Arrays) optimization for homogeneous object arrays:
- doc_read_column_f64: reads entire number column in ONE WASM call
- doc_read_column_bool: reads entire boolean column in ONE WASM call
- doc_read_column_str: reads string ptr/len pairs in ONE WASM call
- Row view with defineProperty getters (lazy class, no Proxy on rows)
- Columns materialize lazily on first property access

Results (1000 items):
- 1 number prop:    0.78x (22% FASTER than JSON.parse)
- 2 number props:   0.97x (3% faster)
- 3 non-str props:  0.96x (4% faster)
- All 5 props:      1.36x (strings still per-element)

Remove eager materialization path, keep cursor-only lazy parsing

Cursor/flyweight pattern beats eager in all cases:
- cursor(5 props): 1.36x vs JSON.parse (was 10x for eager)
- cursor(2 props): ~1.0x (tied with JSON.parse)
- cursor(1 prop): 0.82x (18% FASTER than JSON.parse)

Removed: keyIntern, shapeCtors, getShapeCtor, materializeFromBuffer,
readValue, readUtf16String, compareKeyBytes, readKeyAndRecord,
doc_materialize types, and all eager-mode branching in parse().

Add string key interning and shape-aware constructor caching

Intern repeated object keys so V8/JSC uses pointer comparison for
hidden class transitions. Cache a dynamically-generated constructor
per unique key set — V8 pre-allocates in-object property slots,
eliminating per-property hidden class transitions. Beats JSON.parse
on heterogeneous data (0.90x) and reduces materialization overhead
to near-zero on all workloads.

Fix proxy has-trap and writeStringToMemory memory leak

The proxy `has` handler didn't report "free" or Symbol.dispose as
existing properties, so `'free' in proxy` returned false — causing
doc slot exhaustion and eventual RangeError crash from negative WASM
pointers. Also fix writeStringToMemory to dealloc the full allocated
size (maxBytes) instead of the shorter written length.

Retain materialization buffer, single-pass UTF-8→UTF-16, SIMD widening

doc_materialize_free now just resets mat_len — buffer capacity retained
across eager parses (same pattern as doc_free parser retention).

Eliminated double-scan in string conversion: matWriteStringUtf16 now
allocates upper-bound space, converts in one pass, then patches the
char_count header. Removed utf8Utf16Len from all materialization and
string export paths.

Added SIMD128 fast path in utf8ToUtf16Le: processes 16 ASCII bytes at
once when all are < 0x80 (the common case for JSON keys/values).

JS side: cached full-memory DataView across parses (recreated only on
WASM memory growth), small-string optimization reads char-by-char via
DataView.getUint16 instead of creating Uint16Array views.

Retain parser buffers across parses and pool input buffers

doc_free no longer calls deinit — parser tape, string buffer, and
stack arrays are retained and reused by parseFromSlice automatically.
Add persistent input buffer with doubling growth (ensureInputBuffer)
to eliminate per-parse WASM page allocator round-trips. Switch all
encode paths (parse, writeStringToMemory, validate, deepCompare) from
encoder.encode to encodeInto to avoid intermediate Uint8Array copies.

perf: eliminate per-frame allocations in NAL parsing and message framing

- Replace parseNalUnits() slice-per-unit with index-based scanNalUnits()
  using reusable Uint32Array buffers (eliminates 180-300 allocs/sec at 60fps)
- Pool position array in convertAnnexBToAvcc() with static Uint32Array
- Pool frameWithFlag() buffer in MuxClient (reused across ws.send() calls)
- Use subarray views instead of slice for SPS/PPS codec detection

perf: reduce latency in zstd, control batching, and WebGPU render path

- Reuse WASM src/dst buffers in zstd compress/decompress (0 mallocs steady-state)
- Replace rAF with queueMicrotask for control message batching (~0-1ms vs 0-16ms)
- Pre-allocate WebGPU render pass descriptor outside render loop
- Simplify decodePreviewFrame copy with Uint8Array.slice()

fix: default session role to admin, allow non-admins to fetch own session

The default session was created with editor role, preventing the server
owner from accessing admin features like session management. Share URL
was stuck loading for non-admins because get_session_list silently
rejected non-admin requests.

fix: memory leaks and thread safety in tmux API handlers

- percentDecode: free buf on append/toOwnedSlice failure instead of leaking
- handleTmuxListPanes: free buf on toOwnedSlice failure and on empty-list path
- handleTmuxDisplayMessage: hold mutex for entire function to avoid reading
  Panel pointer fields after unlock (use-after-free if panel freed by another thread)
- split-window: read parent panel fields inside lock scope with defer unlock,
  re-lookup panel inside lock instead of using stale pointer after unlock

fix: jsonFindValue matching key names inside string values

jsonFindValue scanned for "key" patterns anywhere in JSON, including
inside string values. If a value happened to equal a key name (e.g.,
{"val":"cmd","cmd":"real"}), the value match would shadow the real key,
causing jsonGetString/jsonGetInt to return null.

Fix: require a colon after the matched "key" quote. String values are
followed by , or } (not :), so they are now correctly skipped.

Also replace magic keycode 0x0024 in sendCommandToPanel with a
tmux_key_map.get("Enter") lookup for consistency with the key table.

- Remove redundant "JSON extraction for tmux API bodies" test (already

jsonGetString/jsonGetInt/jsonGetArray tests. Merge integration tests
(split-window/new-window/send-keys body parsing) into a single test.
Combine all tmuxKeyLookup tests (named keys, navigation, C-letter,

in handleTmuxDisplayMessage. Consolidate 12 individual interpolation

refactor: extract interpolateTmuxFormat helper, deduplicate format logic

Extract shared interpolateTmuxFormat() from handleTmuxListPanes and
handleTmuxDisplayMessage, fixing display-message missing pane_index,
pane_active, pane_title, pane_current_command support. Remove redundant
percentDecode fast path. Add 21 new tests for format interpolation,

fix: add URL percent-decoding for tmux API query params + unit tests

The tmux shim sends literal #{pane_id} but raw curl sends %23%7Bpane_id%7D.
Without percent-decoding, the server couldn't match #{...} patterns from
URL-encoded requests, causing format interpolation to silently fail.

Add percentDecode() to decode query param values in handleTmuxQuery().
Add ~25 unit tests covering percentDecode, hexDigit, JSON parsing edge
cases, handleTmuxPost routing, send-keys args, and all C-a..C-z ctrl keys.
Tighten integration test assertion to validate actual interpolation result.

fix: replace @enumFromInt with safe intToEnum on untrusted input

@enumFromInt panics on invalid values. Client-supplied bytes on the
panel WebSocket (ClientMsg), auth messages (Role), and corrupted state
files (TransferDirection) could all trigger crashes. Use
std.meta.intToEnum which returns an error instead.

Also clamp uint16 buffer stats to 0xFFFF to prevent silent wraparound.

fix: OAuth set/remove config messages routed to wrong handler

Messages 0x8E/0x8F fell in the 0x80-0x8F range dispatched to
handleBinaryControlMessageFromClient, but the switch cases were in
handleAuthMessage (0x90-0x9F range). This caused admin OAuth config
changes from the UI to silently fail. Reassign to 0x9E/0x9F so they
route correctly.

Rename had_keypress to had_input and set it on both key and scroll
events. This gives scroll the same ~5ms frame pull-back that keypress
already had, reducing scroll-to-display latency from up to 33ms.

Fix WASM pointer overflow for >2GB memory, raise stream limit to 1GB

Three bugs found via 1GB stress testing:

1. WASM i32→u32 pointer overflow: all WASM exports return i32, but
   memory pointers become negative in JS when WASM grows past 2GB.
   Fix: apply >>> 0 to all pointer-returning WASM calls.

2. Stream buffer capped at 64MB: createParser() streaming failed on
   inputs >64MB. Raised MAX_BUFFER_SIZE to 1GB.

3. String encoder over-allocated 3x: writeToWasm() assumed worst-case
   UTF-8 (3 bytes/char) for all strings. Now tries 1x first (optimistic
   for ASCII), falls back to 3x only when multi-byte chars detected.

Verified: 100MB+ parses correctly in all modes (parse, streaming,
event parser). 500MB works in isolation. WASM 4GB address space is
the hard ceiling for sequential large parses.

Add live document builder for O(n) incremental getValue()

object that grows incrementally on each feed(), so getValue() returns
the object reference in O(1) instead of re-parsing the full buffer.
This changes the algorithmic complexity from O(n²) to O(n) total,
yielding 900-2000× speedup over stock SDK parsers at 100KB.

Add generic type inference to createParser() and parsePartialJson()

from Zod, Valibot, ArkType, or any compatible library:

  schema failure (same as incomplete — stream hasn't produced valid data)
- parsePartialJson<T>(input, schema): returns DeepPartial<T> with a
  discriminated union on state for type narrowing. Repaired-parse keeps
  the raw partial value when validation fails (missing fields expected).

  the value type automatically

perf: inline JSValueNative dup/free to avoid C FFI (~5% CPU improvement)

Inline ref_count increment in dup() and fast-path decrement in free()
directly in Zig, matching CompressedValue.dupRef/freeRef pattern. Only
calls C JS_FreeValue for actual destruction (ref_count reaches 0).
Eliminates cross-compilation-unit call overhead for the most frequent
operations. Binary grows only 1.5MB (196MB vs 194.5MB).

compiler to inline ref_count increments and pointer dereferences
directly, eliminating cross-compilation-unit call overhead.

every function call, CV conversion, and ref counting operation.

Results: EdgeBox TSC user time drops from ~14s to ~3.8s, matching
Node.js V8 (3.73s) on TypeScript type-checking benchmarks.

perf(freeze): add CV.dupRef/freeRef for direct ref counting (~7.3% faster)

Replace CV→JSValue→C FFI→JSValue→CV roundtrip for ref counting with
direct pointer arithmetic on JSRefCountHeader. dupRef increments ref_count
at offset 0 of the pointed-to object; freeRef decrements and only falls
back to JS_FreeValue when ref_count reaches 0.

Both functions are noinline, replacing ~50 inlined instructions per call
site with a single function call — reducing code size across 881 shards
and improving icache behavior.

Results on TSC benchmark:
- Call cycles: 11,087M → 10,280M (↓ 7.3%)
- CV-to-JSValue conversions: 126M → 64.7M (↓ 48.7%)

revert: remove sentinel pre-check for frame hashing

Sampling 8 pixels to skip the full hash is fundamentally unreliable —
changes that don't land on a sampled position get missed, delaying
frame delivery. The full XxHash64 hash is always correct and the
~1-2ms cost is acceptable for guaranteed frame accuracy.

fix: use panel.had_input instead of stale atomic for input detection

The has_pending_input atomic is cleared by processInputQueue() before
the frame capture loop runs, so it was always false. Use panel.had_input
which is set during processInputQueue and persists until after frame
capture — this is the flag designed for exactly this purpose.

Without this fix, the sentinel pre-check never detected input, causing
typed text to appear with ~1s delay until something else triggered a
full hash.

fix: bypass sentinel pre-check when input is pending

Typed characters may not land on any of the 8 sentinel pixel positions,
causing false "unchanged" detection and delaying frame delivery by up to
a second. Always compute the full hash when has_pending_input is set.

perf: remove runtime base-offset checks from pointer compression

Since the low-address arena guarantees all pointers are within 44-bit
range (base=0), the runtime getCompressedHeapBase() load + compare +
branch in compressPtr/decompressPtr/toJSValue is dead code. Remove it
entirely — compress is now a single AND, decompress is a single mask.
initCompressedHeap() still panics if base != 0 as a safety check.

perf: wire IC into switch-dispatch get_field/get_field2 fallback paths

The IC struct was declared but unused — get_field/get_field2 still used

perf: revert extern var to pub var for zero-cost pointer compression

The extern var added 3 extra instructions (memory load + compare + branch)
on every compressPtr/decompressPtr — millions of times per second in TSC.
Since the low-address arena already works (heap_base=0x0, arena at 64GB
within 44-bit range), LLVM can constant-fold the entire check to nothing.
This matches macOS ARM64 behavior: compressPtr becomes a single AND.

Panic at startup if arena is outside 44-bit range as a safety guard.

perf: add inline cache (IC) to switch-dispatch frozen codegen

The switch-dispatch codegen (zig_codegen_full.zig) was the only codegen
path without inline caching for property access. Every get_field/get_field2
opcode went through full C FFI (JS_GetPropertyStr → atom hash lookup →
shape/property walk) on every call.

With IC enabled, the first access populates a shape+offset cache. Subsequent
accesses with matching shape do a single pointer dereference in Zig — zero
C FFI. This is critical for TSC which does millions of property accesses
(.kind, .flags, .pos, .end, .parent, etc.) on AST nodes.

perf: allocate arena in low 44-bit address range for zero-cost pointer compression

On Linux x86_64, request arena at hint address 0x10_0000_0000 (64GB) so
it falls within the 44-bit PTR_ADDR_MASK. When successful, compressed_heap_base
stays 0 and compressPtr/decompressPtr use the fast path (addr & MASK) with
no extern var access — matching macOS ARM64 performance characteristics.

perf: use extern var for compressed_heap_base instead of C function call

Replace edgebox_get_compressed_heap_base() function call with direct
extern var access. Eliminates function call overhead on every pointer
compress/decompress operation (~11% faster: 7.8s → 6.9s on TSC).
Also remove debug panic from compressPtr fallback path.

fix: frozen interpreter crash on Linux x86_64

Root cause: CompressedValue's 44-bit pointer compression uses a heap_base
variable to compute pointer offsets. Each frozen shard (733 for TSC) is
compiled as a separate Zig object file. LLVM inlined the heap_base variable
into each shard, constant-folding it to 0, so the arena compression path
was never taken and pointers above 44 bits were silently truncated.

Fix: Move compressed_heap_base to a C global in profile_counters.c (which
is compiled once and linked into the native binary). Frozen shards access
it via extern C functions (edgebox_get/set_compressed_heap_base), ensuring
a single shared instance.

Also: contiguous arena allocator using mmap virtual reservation (8GB) on
Linux to keep all QuickJS heap allocations within a single address range.

fix: use C extern for compressed_heap_base across frozen shards

Each frozen shard is compiled as a separate Zig compilation unit with
its own module instance. A Zig 'pub var' gets duplicated per unit, and
LLVM constant-folds each copy to 0. Even 'volatile' doesn't help because
the variable is still local to each shard.

Solution: define the variable in C (frozen_dispatch_cli.c) and reference
it as 'extern var edgebox_compressed_heap_base' in Zig. This forces all
compilation units to use the single global instance, which is set by
initCompressedHeap() at runtime before any frozen code executes.

fix: use volatile read for compressed_heap_base in frozen shards

Each frozen shard is a separate Zig compilation unit. Without volatile,
LLVM sees compressed_heap_base = 0 at compile time and constant-folds
the arena compression path away. The volatile load forces a runtime read,
ensuring all shards use the actual heap base set by initCompressedHeap().

fix(freeze): dup locals in relooper flushVstack to prevent use-after-free

The relooper's flushVstack was copying locals[N] to the physical stack
without duping ref-counted values. The full codegen's materializeVStack
correctly dups storage references (locals[], stack[]) when flushing, but
the relooper's equivalent did a raw assignment. This caused the physical
stack and locals to share a single refcount — when either path freed the
value, the other became a dangling pointer.
